questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Ramon Magsaysay Award, consider the following statements:
I. - It is given in six fixed award categories, including Community Leadership and Journalism.
II. 	 It is bestowed only for the service to the people
of Asia.
III. 	 ‘Educate Girls’ became the first Indian NGO to
be honoured with this award in 2025.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Ramon Magsaysay Award** was started in 1958 to celebrate “greatness of spirit and transformative leadership in Asia”.  The award was set up in 1957 by trustees of the **Rockefeller Brothers Fund**, created by the wealthy Rockefeller family in the United States, and the **Philippine government**. The award is given out every year on August 31, on the former Philippine President Ramon Magsaysay’s birth anniversary.
● From 1958 to 2008, the award was given in six categories annually. **Starting in 2009, the award is no** **longer being given in fixed award categories, except for Emergent Leadership.** The winners are presented with a medallion, a certificate, and a cash prize of about USD 50,000. **So, statement I is not** **correct.**
● The award is widely considered to be Asia’s equivalent to the Nobel Prize. It recognises outstanding leadership and communitarian contributions in Asia. It honours individuals and organisations demonstrating **exceptional courage and selfless service to the people of Asia only**. **So, statement II is** **correct.**
● **NGO ‘Educate Girls’ won the Ramon Magsaysay Award 2025.** **It became the first Indian** **organisation to receive the award**. Educate Girls is an Indian non-profit working to bring out-of-school girls into the classroom. It has been awarded for “its commitment to addressing cultural stereotyping through the education of girls and young women, liberating them from the bondage of illiteracy and infusing them with skills, courage and agency to achieve their full human potential.” **So, statement III is** **correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● Educate Girls, also known as Foundation to Educate Girls Globally, is one of the three winners of the 2025 Ramon Magsaysay Award.
● The other winners are **Shaahina Ali of the Maldives and Flaviano Antonio L.** **Villanueva of the Philippines.** Shaahina Ali was awarded for her commitment to protecting the marine ecosystem of the Maldives. Villanueva was recognised for his lifelong mission to uphold the dignity of the poor and the oppressed.
● India has produced over 50 Ramon Magsaysay laureates since 1958, the second-highest after the Philippines. Past Indian recipients of this award include Vinoba Bhave (1958) for the Bhoodan movement, Mother Teresa (1962) for humanitarian work, Satyajit Ray (1967), Arvind Kejriwal, journalist Ravish Kumar, activist Bezwada Wilson, and environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk.
**Relevance:** Indian NGO ‘Educate Girls’ won the Ramon Magsaysay Award 2025.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText: In South India, farmers apply Bordeaux mixture to the coffee plants after harvest because:

options.a:
It accelerates nutrient uptake and promotes fresh
vegetative growth immediately after harvest
options.b:
It improves soil aeration and reduces the acidity
caused by continuous cropping
options.c:
It prevents wilting of plants by retaining
moisture in stems and leaves
options.d:
It protects against fungal diseases during wet
conditions

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Bordeaux** mixture is a fungicide product resulting from the reaction of copper sulfate and calcium hydroxide. **It is primarily used to control various fungal and bacterial diseases in plants**. It controls many fungal and bacterial leaf spots, blights, anthracnoses, downy mildews and cankers.
● **As it has a low aqueous solubility and is not volatile, it persists in rainy weather** and can provide longlasting protection against a variety of diseases.
● Recent rainfalls in Karnataka have heightened fears of rot disease in coffee and areca crops. Planters there are gearing up to apply Bordeaux mixture for the third or fourth time this year, following earlier crop damage caused by unseasonal rainfall. Unseasonal rainfall creates a favourable environment for the disease to spread. Excessive rainfall causes water to stagnate, and this excessive moisture spreads the rot disease. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, continuous rainfall in Chikkamagaluru and Hassan districts of Karnataka has heightened fears of rot disease in coffee and areca crops.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Financial Markets (Money Market, Capital Market, SEBI)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. - Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
II. 	 Pension Fund Regulatory and Development
Authority (PFRDA)
III. 	 Insurance Regulatory Development Authority
of India (IRDAI)
IV. 	 Competition Commission of India (CCI) The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) hears appeals against the orders of how many of the above bodies?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT)** is a statutory body established under the provisions of Section 15K of the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 to hear and dispose of appeals against orders passed by the **Securities and Exchange Board of India** **(SEBI)** or by an adjudicating officer under the Act; and to exercise jurisdiction, powers and authority conferred on the Tribunal by or under this Act or any other law for the time being in force. **So, point I is correct.**
● Consequent to a Government Notification in 2014, SAT hears and disposes of appeals against orders passed by the **Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)** under the PFRDA Act, 2013. **So, point II is correct.**
● Further, in terms of Government Notification in 2015, SAT hears and disposes of appeals against orders passed by the **Insurance Regulatory Development Authority of India (IRDAI)** under the Insurance Act, 1938, the General Insurance Business (Nationalization) Act, 1972, and the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act, 1999, and the Rules and Regulations framed thereunder. **So, point III is** **correct.**
● Appeals against the orders of the **Competition Commission of India** (CCI) are heard by the **National** **Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT)**. **So, point IV is not correct.** **So, the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) hears appeals against the orders of only three of the above** **bodies.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, a US-based trading firm, Jane Street Group, filed an appeal in the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) against the SEBI in the alleged market manipulation case.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText: Which one of the following species has been observed to bring gifts of freshly caught prey to humans and seemingly wait for a response or reward?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A new study has found that **Killer Whales may be exploring human behaviour** by **offering them whole** **prey and waiting for them to respond**. Many members of the largest species of dolphins, commonly called Killer Whales (Orcinus orca), have been found sharing freshly killed prey with humans.
● These Killer Whales are found to offer diverse prey such as seaweed, invertebrates, and fish. The researchers said that the Killer Whales may have been exploring. Animals mainly explore their surroundings to reduce uncertainty about their physical, social, and/or environmental surroundings.
● **Killer Whale:** ○ It is the ocean’s top predator and the **largest member of the Dolphin Family**. They are the most widely distributed of all dolphins. ○ They are known to be **prosocial animals** and among the few that share prey both among themselves and across species. ○ They have high levels of encephalisation, meaning **a larger brain size relative to the size of the** **body**. ○ These animals live and **hunt in groups** led by a matriarch, the oldest female, and the group’s behaviour largely depends on the matriarch. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A new study has found that killer whales may be exploring human behaviour by offering them whole prey and waiting for them to respond.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText: ‘PRATUSH’, a digital receiver system, has been developed to:

options.a:
Establish a secure quantum communication
pathway
options.b:
Accurately map the global oceanic and glacial
ecosystems
options.c:
Enable a high-resolution weather forecasting
model
options.d: Detect and study the cosmological signals

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Probing Reionization of the Universe using Signal from Hydrogen (PRATUSH)** is a radio telescope designed to be placed on the far side of the moon. It is a **radiometer proposed to be installed in lunar** **orbit**.
○ It will **detect the faint cosmological signal**. The radio signals will be captured by the antenna, amplified by the analog receiver, and turned into digital data by the digital receiver. It can help detect a faint radio signal emitted from hydrogen atoms (21-cm signal), which carries imprints of several events of the Cosmic Dawn.
○ PRATUSH will answer the question of **when the first stars formed in our universe**, the nature of the first stars, and what the light from the first stars or, in other words, the **colour of the light** **of Cosmic Dawn**. It will be the pioneering space telescope that will reveal the history of our infant Universe as it transformed after the Big Bang.
● PRATUSH is designed by a team from **Raman Research Institute (RRI)**, an autonomous institute funded by the Department of Science and Technology. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Scientists at the Raman Research Institute have proposed a pioneering mission called PRATUSH, designed to unlock the Cosmic Dawn.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText: Recently, scientists have recommended the use of ‘Clopidogrel’ over aspirin for:

options.a: Prevention of the formation of blood clots

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Clopidogrel** is in a class of medications called antiplatelet medications. Clopidogrel is a **medication that** **prevents blood clots** by making platelets less sticky, thereby **reducing the chances of heart attacks and** **strokes**.
● A recent study found that **clopidogrel outperforms aspirin in preventing heart attacks** and strokes. Aspirin is a blood thinner that can help prevent heart attacks and strokes.
● The study suggests that clopidogrel should be considered the preferred long-term antiplatelet therapy for those with coronary artery disease, a condition that affects millions worldwide.
● Bleeding is one of the biggest safety concerns when prescribing any long-term blood thinner like aspirin. Clopidogrel can offer superior protection without increasing bleeding risks. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** New study finds clopidogrel outperforms aspirin in preventing heart attacks and strokes.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText: The discovery of jarosite in Kutch, Gujarat, suggests which one of the following similarities between Earth and Mars?

options.b:
Existence of similar surface oxidation and
weathering processes

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Jarosite:** It is a yellow-coloured iron-rich sulphate mineral. Its formation is typically associated with volcanic activity. On Earth, it is formed when some minerals containing oxygen, iron, sulphur, and potassium interact in the right quantities in the presence of water.
● A joint team of researchers from the **Space Applications Centre** (Ahmedabad), **Savitribai Phule Pune** **University,** and the **Birbal Sahni Institute of Palaeosciences** (Lucknow) has confirmed that **jarosite** **(yellow-coloured iron-rich sulphate mineral),** first identified in **Matanomadh (village in Gujarat’s** **Kutch district) in 2016**, is about **55 million years old**, dating back to the **Paleocene epoch**.
● This discovery points to a geological process that occurred **both on Earth and Mars millions of years** **ago.** The **environmental and chemical conditions of Matanomadh** are therefore considered **analogous** **to those on Mars,** making the site a promising field-analogue for future Mars missions.
○ Here, scientists can test rover mobility, drilling techniques, instrument performance, and conduct geochemical and mineralogical studies to better understand the Martian surface and its potential biochemistry. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, researchers have confirmed the presence of jarosite, a yellow iron-rich sulphate mineral, akin to the one identified on Mars nearly two decades ago.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to the coconut crop:
I. 	 It is a temperate crop and can thrive in
continuous high humidity.
II. 	 It grows well in all types of soils, including
sandy and saline.
III. 	India is the second largest exporter of
coconuts in the world after Indonesia.
IV. 	 Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh
are the major coconut-producing States in India.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Coconut palm** thrives in diverse climatic and soil conditions but is essentially a **tropical crop,** predominantly grown between **20°N and 20°S latitudes.** The ideal conditions for its growth include a mean temperature of 27 ± 5°C and relative humidity above 60%. However, **continuously high humidity** **is not favourable.** The crop grows well up to 600 m above mean sea level, though near the equator, productive plantations can be established even at elevations of about 1000 m. **So, statement I is not** **correct.**
● The cultivation of coconut has slowly extended to inland areas, even to the hill tops, having varied soil conditions. It **grows well in almost all types of soils,** including **sandy,** laterite, swampy, alluvial, black, and **saline soils,** provided they have a proper drainage system, permitting unrestricted root development, aeration, and absence of rock or a hard substratum within 2 m of the surface. It tolerates salinity and a wide pH range from 5.0 to 8.0. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **Philippines is the largest coconut-exporting country**, accounting for **20%** of global exports. It is followed by **Indonesia** with **18%** and **India** with **15%**. **Brazil** contributes **12%**, while **Vietnam** has a smaller share at **3%**. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● India is the **world’s largest producer of coconuts**, contributing 30.93% of global output, followed by Indonesia and the Philippines. In terms of productivity, India ranks second with 9,346 nuts per hectare, next only to Vietnam’s 10,547 nuts per hectare. The four southern states, viz., **Kerala, Tamil Nadu,** **Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh,** are the major coconut-producing states in India, accounting for **more** **than 90 per cent of the area and production. So, statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **Coconut Development Board** is a **statutory body** established by the Government of India for the integrated development of coconut production and utilisation in the country, with a focus on productivity increase and product diversification.
● It came into existence in **1981** and functions under the administrative control of the **Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare,** Government of India, with its Headquarters at **Kochi in Kerala** and Regional Offices at Bangalore in Karnataka, Chennai in Tamil Nadu, Guwahati in Assam, and Patna in Bihar.
**Relevance:** Recently, the Coconut Development Board (CDB) has celebrated World Coconut Day by launching its newly revised schemes.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
India s first fully indigenous 32-bit microprocessor, “Vikram," has applications in which of the following sectors?
I. 	 Navigation systems
II. 	 Spacecraft avionics
III. 	Defence systems
IV. 	 Smart energy meters
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Vikram-32 (Vikram 3201)** is India’s first fully **indigenously designed** and manufactured **32-bit** **microprocessor.** Developed by the Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre of Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and fabricated at the Semiconductor Laboratory, Mohali, it is intended for use in rockets, satellites, and other mission-critical aerospace systems.
● The Vikram 3201 microprocessor is designed to manage **navigation system, control, and mission** **operations** in launch vehicles, executing real-time calculations essential for maintaining **rocket stability** **and trajectory.** With the ability to handle substantial memory and execute complex instructions, it is well-suited for the demands of **satellite launches and other critical space missions. So, points I and II** **are correct.**
● Engineered to **military-grade standards,** it undergoes rigorous testing to ensure reliability under the harsh conditions of space. The chip can withstand extreme temperatures ranging from **–55°C to +125°C,**
**intense vibrations, and cosmic radiation,** making it ideal for **aerospace and defence. So, point III is** **correct.**
● It is not intended for commercial applications like Smart energy meters, which require different design priorities. **So, point IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, India unveiled its first fully indigenous 32-bit microprocessor, the Vikram 3201, at the Semicon India 2025 conference.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Wildlife Conservation (Tiger, Elephant, Lion, Rhino, Crocodile, Vulture, Tortoise, Dolphin, etc.)
questions.questionText:
“It is the most trafficked mammal in the world. It
rolls up into a ball when threatened and is called a scaly anteater due to its diet of ants and termites”.
Which mammal is this?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Pangolins,** or scaly anteaters, are extraordinary animals. They are the only mammals wholly covered in scales, and they use those scales to protect themselves from predators in the wild.
● There are 8 species of pangolin; 4 are found in Asia and 4 are found in Africa. All species are listed in  **Appendix** **I by CITES**, the same level of protection as the rhinoceros.
● If under threat, a pangolin will immediately **curl into a** **tight ball** and use its sharp-scaled tail to defend itself.
● Pangolins are the **most trafficked mammals in the** **world,** with most trafficking in African species smuggled to Asia. Pangolins are in high demand in countries like China and Vietnam.
● Their meat is considered a delicacy, and pangolin scales are used in traditional medicine. All eight IUCNrecognized pangolin species are protected under national and international laws.
● In 2016, a treaty of over 180 governments announced an agreement that would end all legal trade of pangolins and further protect the species from extinction.
● They **eat ants, termites, and larvae** and are often known as "**the scaly anteater."** Because they have no teeth, pangolins pick up food with their sticky tongues, which can sometimes reach lengths greater than the animal’s body. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Subject:** Environment|Biodiversity and Conservation
**Relevance:** A pangolin was poached at Tipeshwar Wildlife Sanctuary, leading to the arrest of five individuals who were subsequently placed in forest custody.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Water body Connects
I. - Strait of Malacca
Andaman Sea and South China Sea
II. - Taiwan Strait
East China Sea and South China Sea
III. Strait of
Gibraltar
Atlantic Ocean and Mediterranean Sea
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Strait of Malacca:** It is a narrow waterway between the Malay Peninsula and the Indonesian island of Sumatra. It connects the **Andaman Sea (Indian Ocean) with the South China Sea (Pacific Ocean)**, making it one of the world’s busiest maritime trade routes. It is strategically vital for global oil shipments, especially for China, Japan, and India. **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● **Taiwan Strait:** It separates the island of Taiwan from mainland China and connects the **South China Sea** **with the East China Sea**. It holds major geopolitical importance due to tensions between China and Taiwan. It is also a critical shipping lane for global trade and semiconductor supply chains. **So, pair II is** **correctly matched.**
● **Strait of Gibraltar:** It lies between southern Spain and northern Morocco, linking the **Atlantic Ocean** **with the Mediterranean Sea**. It is a key chokepoint for maritime traffic between Europe, Africa, and the Middle East. The British Overseas Territory of Gibraltar sits at its northern entrance. **So, pair III is** **correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, India secured Singapore’s support for its interest in patrolling the Malacca Strait.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Recently, ESA’s Solar Orbiter traced the origin
of energy particles from the Sun. What is the significance of this observation?

options.a:
It confirmed the existence of wormholes in
interplanetary space
options.b:
It enabled harnessing of solar particles for
interplanetary fuel
options.c:
It detected the Higgs boson particle in solar
plasma
options.d:
It could help scientists in predicting and
understanding space weather

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **European Space Agency’s (ESA) Solar Orbiter,** launched in 2020 in collaboration with **NASA,** has provided significant insights into the Sun’s behaviour. The Orbiter has traced the origin of **energetic** **particles such as high-energy electrons**, linking them directly to solar events like **flares and Coronal** **Mass Ejections (CMEs).** These particles play a crucial role in **shaping space weather,** which refers to the **dynamic space environment** influenced by solar activity, including **solar winds, flares, CMEs, and** **particle storms.**
● Understanding the source and acceleration of these particles is vital for predicting and mitigating space weather hazards, which can:
○ Damage or disrupt satellites, GPS, and communication networks ○ Causes voltage instabilities and blackouts in power grids on Earth ○ Pose serious risks to astronauts and future space missions.
● The Solar Orbiter is also unique because it has provided the **closest-ever images of the Sun and is the** **first mission to observe its polar regions**, both of which are critical for advancing heliophysics.
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the European Space Agency’s Solar Orbiter has achieved a breakthrough by tracing the origins of energetic particles from the Sun.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following scenario:
A human-rights organisation discovers that several activists’ Android phones have been compromised. The malicious code was hidden inside a widely used messaging app, allowing the attacker to read photos and encrypted chats, track real-time location, effectively providing near-total remote control of the device. The malware is reported to be used against journalists and civil-society actors, despite claims of ethical deployment by its vendors.
Which one of the following malware is described above?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Graphite** is designed to gain **remote access to a mobile phone and essentially take control of it**. The user of the spyware can not only access the mobile user’s photos, read their messages, and track their whereabouts, but also **monitor encrypted messages** sent on platforms such as **WhatsApp and Signal**.
● It targets Android devices by embedding itself **within legitimate apps like WhatsApp, allowing near-** **total control and covert data extraction**. Despite claims of ethical use, Graphite has been reported to **target journalists and civil society members worldwide, including India**. The spyware also enables the phone to be used as a listening device by manipulating its recorder. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the US administration has unfrozen a stalled Biden-era contract with Paragon Solutions, a Graphite spyware company founded in Israel.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Security and Strategic Issues (Terrorism, Cyber, Maritime, Exercises, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which of the following are domestic military
exercises conducted by the defence forces of India?
I. 	 Exercise Yudh Kaushal
II. 	 Exercise Yudh Abhyas
III. 	Exercise MAITREE
IV. 	 Exercise ZAPAD
V. 	 Exercise Siyom Prahar
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Exercise Yudh Kaushal** is a **domestic military exercise** conducted by the Indian Army to enhance tactical and operational skills. It focuses on combat readiness and coordination among units. **So, point I** **is correct.**
● **Exercise Yudh Abhyas** is a bilateral military exercise between **India and the United States**, aimed at improving interoperability and joint combat capabilities. **So, point II is not correct.**
● **Exercise MAITREE** is a bilateral army exercise between **India and Thailand**, focusing on counterinsurgency and counter-terrorism operations. **So, point III is not correct.**
● **Exercise ZAPAD** is a multinational strategic military exercise hosted by **Russia**, in which India has participated as an observer or limited participant. **So, point IV is not correct.**
● **Exercise Siyom Prahar** is a major field training exercise **conducted by the Indian Army to validate** **and integrate drone technology** into tactical combat operations. **So, point V is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Exercise Siyom Prahar was held in Arunachal Pradesh from September 8–10, 2025.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Hydroelectric Project River
I. - Dhauliganga Hydroelectric Project
Wangchu River
II. - Ratle Hydroelectric Project
Sutlej River
III. - Nathpa Jhakri Hydroelectric Project
Chenab River
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Dhauliganga Hydroelectric Project (Uttarakhand):** It is a run-of-the-river scheme on the **Dhauliganga** **River, a tributary of the Kali River.** It is operated by NHPC Limited and plays a key role in meeting regional power needs and flood control in the Himalayan foothills. **So, pair I is not correctly matched.**
● **Ratle Hydroelectric Project (Jammu & Kashmir):** It is an 850 MW hydroelectric project being developed jointly by NHPC Limited and Jammu & Kashmir State Power Development Corporation on the **Chenab River**. It is a run-of-the-river project crucial for harnessing the Chenab basin’s potential and ensuring a reliable power supply in northern India. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● At 1,500 MW capacity, **Nathpa Jhakri is the largest hydropower project on the Sutlej River** in the state of Himachal Pradesh. The project is owned by the SJVN Ltd, a joint venture between the government
of India and the government of Himachal Pradesh. The government of India envisages a total of around 30 projects on the Sutlej River. **So, pair III is not correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A major landslide struck the vital Rattle hydroelectric project in Kishtwar recently.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Transport (Roadways, Railways, Waterways, Ports, Airways, Pipelines, etc.)
questions.questionText:
The recently inaugurated Bairabi–Sairang Rail
Line project is the first-ever railway line through
which one of the following States?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Bairabi–Sairang broad-gauge project is Mizoram’s first-ever railway line**. It marks a historic moment in the State’s connectivity journey. Among its most striking structures is Bridge No. 144 near Sairang, which at 114 metres **stands taller than Qutub Minar,** making it th**e tallest pier railway bridge** **in the country**.
● The new route extends connectivity from **Bairabi on the Assam–Mizoram border to Sairang**, just 20 km from Aizawl, directly linking the state capital with India’s railway network for the first time. With this, **Aizawl becomes the fourth northeastern capital to gain direct rail access**, after Assam's Guwahati, Tripura's Agartala, and Arunachal Pradesh's Itanagar. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Prime Minister of India recently inaugurated Mizoram’s first-ever railway line, the Bairabi– Sairang broad-gauge project.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Physiography of India (Location, Division, Physiographic Regions)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Lake Description
I. - Great Salt Lake Largest saltwater lake in the world
II. - Lake Baikal Largest freshwater lake by volume in the world
III. Tso Kar Lake Highest navigable lake in the world
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Great Salt Lake (USA):** It is located in Utah and is the **largest saltwater lake in the Western** **Hemisphere**. The lake supports unique ecosystems, including brine shrimp and migratory birds. Its fluctuating water levels affect the local climate and industry, particularly salt extraction and mineral production. The **Caspian Sea is the largest saltwater lake in the world**. **So, pair I is not correctly** **matched.**
● The **largest freshwater lake in the world by volume is Lake Baikal in Russia**, which contains about 20% of the world's unfrozen surface freshwater. It is located in the southern part of eastern Siberia within the republic of Buryatia and Irkutsk oblast (province) of Russia. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Lake Titicaca** is a large freshwater lake in the Andes mountains on the border of Bolivia and Peru. It is the **highest navigable lake in the world**, situated at an elevation of about 3,812 meters (12,507 feet) above sea level. **So, pair III is not correctly matched.** **Tso Kar:** Known as the ‘White Lake’ due to its high salt content, Tso Kar is located at around 4,530 m in the Rupshu Valley. Unlike Pangong and Tso Moriri, Tso Kar has a unique ecological system since it **combines** **freshwater and saltwater.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Human activity over the past 200 years has pushed Utah's Great Salt Lake into a biogeochemical state not seen for at least 2,000 years, driven by irrigation and a 1959 railroad causeway.

questions.subject: Miscellaneous
questions.questionText:
Methandienone Long-Term Metabolite (LTM),
recently seen in the news, refers to:

options.a:
Reference material for detecting anabolic
steroid use in sports

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In a significant step towards strengthening anti-doping efforts in sports, the National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (NIPER) Guwahati, under the **Department of Pharmaceuticals,** in collaboration with the **National Dope Testing Laboratory (NDTL)**, New Delhi, has developed a rare and high-purity Reference Material (RM) – **Methandienone Long-Term Metabolite (LTM).**
○ **Reference Materials (RMs) are the most highly purified and scientifically characterized** **forms of drug** substances or their metabolites, essential for **accurate analytical testing**. In the context of anti-doping, they are **crucial for the detection of over 450 substances that are** **currently prohibited by the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA)**. ○ Due to their specialized application in dope testing, these RMs are **produced by only a few** **manufacturers globally (approximately 4–5)**, making them scarce and often expensive. ○ **As of now, Methandienone LTM is not commercially available globally**. In the present scenario, the best targets to track doping misuse in sports are those eliminated in urine over extended periods. These are usually referred to as **long-term metabolites (LTMs)**. ○ These metabolites **help in identifying athletes who have used Methandienone**, even if they **ceased usage months or years prior to testing**. This leads to an increase in the number of positive tests, reflecting **improved detection capabilities and serving as a deterrent** for athletes considering the use of anabolic steroids like Methandienone. ○ The use of this new reference material, “Methandienone LTM,” will help in **enforcing** **transparency and integrity in sports** by protecting clean athletes and acting as a deterrent for the use of prohibited substances. This reference material may be shared with all 30 WADA-accredited laboratories worldwide as India’s contribution to the world anti-doping community. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The National Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (NIPER) Guwahati has developed a rare and high-purity Reference Material (RM) – Methandienone Long-Term Metabolite (LTM).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
What is the role of the p47 protein in the human
body?

options.a: It stores oxygen in muscle cells.
options.b:
It prevents tumor formation by controlling
cell division.
options.c:
It protects cells from mechanical stress by
helping them refold.
options.d:
It provides structure to the connective tissues
of the body.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **p47 protein** acts as a **"mechanical chaperone,"** protecting cells from mechanical stress by binding to stretched proteins and **helping them refold**.
● p47, a cofactor protein usually known **as a helper for the cellular machine p97**. While **p97 is a** **powerhouse involved in moving and degrading proteins**, p47 was long thought to be just an assistant known primarily for its role in protein trafficking, degradation, and membrane fusion.
● Recently, scientists have found that **p47 is not just a passive helper for p97**; it can **directly stabilize** **proteins under force, effectively acting as a “mechanical chaperone.”** In the experiments, p47 bound to mechanically stretched proteins and enhanced their ability to refold, even under constant pulling forces. This mechanical assistance mirrors the foldase-like activity of canonical chaperones but comes from an accessory factor that was not previously thought to possess such a function.
● The study provides **direct, single-molecule evidence that cofactors like p47 can have autonomous,** **force-dependent protective activity**. This discovery opens the door to rethinking the broader roles of accessory proteins in cellular mechanics and protein quality control. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **p53** is a tumor suppressor protein nicknamed the "guardian of the genome" that prevents cancer by controlling cell division and triggering apoptosis (cell death) in response to DNA damage.
● **Collagen** protein provides structure to skin, bones, muscles, and connective tissue.
● **Myoglobin** protein stores oxygen in muscle cells.
**Relevance:** Indian scientists discovered protein p47’s role as a mechanical chaperone, paving the way for new treatments.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With regard to the latest Sample Registration
Survey Statistical Report for 2023, which of the following has shown a declining trend?
I. 	 Crude Birth Rate
II. 	 Total Fertility Rate
III. 	Infant Mortality Rate
IV. 	 Sex Ratio at Birth
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The Office of the Registrar General of India released the **Sample Registration Survey (SRS) Statistical Report** **for 2023**, which highlights a decline in key demographic indicators. The findings of the report are:
● **India’s Crude Birth Rate (CBR)**, the number of children born per 1,000 people in the population in a year, has **declined** by 0.7 points from 19.1 in 2022 to 18.4 in 2023. **So, point I is correct.**
○ The **highest CBR was in Bihar** at 25.8, and the **lowest was in Tamil Nadu** at 12. **Bihar reported** **the highest TFR** (2.8) among the bigger States and Union Territories (UTs), and **Delhi reported** **the lowest** (1.2).
● The country’s **Total Fertility Rate (TFR) has fallen for the first time in two years** to 1.9 in 2023. In 2021 and 2022, India’s TFR remained constant at 2.0. **So, point II is correct.**
○ The report pointed out that 18 States and UTs had reported a TFR of below the replacement level TFR of 2.1.
■ **Replacement level TFR** denotes the average number of children each woman needs to give birth to for one generation to replace the other.
● The country’s **Crude Death Rate** was at 6.4 in 2023, a 0.4-point **decline** from 2022.
● There has been a 1-point **decline in the Infant Mortality Rate** nationally from 2022, and a **3-point rise** **in the national Sex Ratio at Birth** between the 2020-2022 and 2021-2023 periods. **So, point III is** **correct and point IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Sample Registration Survey Statistical Report, 2023, was recently released.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: The Moon appears reddish during a total lunar eclipse.
Statement II: The Earth's atmosphere scatters red and orange light during a total lunar eclipse.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● When **Earth passes directly between the Sun and the Moon** during a **total lunar eclipse,** it is known as a **Blood Moon**. The Moon appears reddish because the **Earth's atmosphere scatters shorter-** **wavelength blue light**, allowing longer-wavelength red and orange light to reach the Moon's surface. **So,** **statement I is correct and statement II is not correct.**
● Sunlight traveling through the atmosphere of our planet is scattered as the Moon is obscured by the Earth's shadow. Longer light wavelengths (orange and red) pass through, whereas shorter ones (violet and blue) are filtered out. Bright sunrises and sunsets are a result of this similar process.
● **Total lunar eclipses don’t happen every year**, and when they do, they are visible only to a portion of the world. Unlike a solar eclipse, which requires protective glasses, a lunar eclipse is completely safe to view with the naked eye. **So, statement I is correct, but statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the blood moon was visible in India.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 The Model List of Essential Medicines (EML)
is updated by the World Health Organisation (WHO) every 10 years.
II. 	 The AWaRe classification system is a tool for
monitoring antibiotic use and reducing the spread of antibiotic resistance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **World Health Organisation (WHO) Essential Medicines List (EML)** is a register of minimum medicine needs for every health-care system. First published in 1977, the **EML is revised every 2 years** **by a committee of experts** from around the world in fields such as medicine, pharmacology, policy, regulation, and health organizations. The expert committee updates the model list to reflect changes in global health concerns, pharmaceutical developments, and patterns of drug resistance. **So, statement I is** **not correct.**
○ The WHO has released an updated (24th) edition of its Model Lists of Essential Medicines (EML) and Essential Medicines for Children (EMLc), adding new treatments for various types of **cancer** **and for diabetes with associated comorbidities such as obesity**.
● The AWaRe classification is intended as a **tool for monitoring antibiotic consumption**, defining targets and monitoring the effects of stewardship policies that aim to optimize antibiotic use and curb **antimicrobial resistance**.
○ As per the classification, antibiotics are classified into three groups, **Access, Watch and Reserve**, taking into account the impact of different antibiotics and antibiotic classes on antimicrobial resistance, to emphasize the importance of their appropriate use. It is **updated every 2 years.** **So,** **statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the World Health Organization (WHO) has released updated editions of its Model Lists of Essential Medicines (EML) and Essential Medicines for Children (EMLc).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
The term Manki–Munda system, recently in the
news, refers to:

options.a:
A hereditary self-governance institution
functioning at the village level
options.c:
A land revenue settlement mechanism
introduced by the British
options.d:
A tribal council formed under the Panchayats
(Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Manki–Munda system** is the **traditional self-governance system** of the **Ho tribe** in Jharkhand’s **Kolhan region**.
● It is a **century-old institution** balancing indigenous autonomy with state administration.
● In the system, each **village head**, known as the **Munda**, is responsible for resolving social and political disputes at the village level. The position of Munda is hereditary and is passed from father to son.
● A **Manki** supervises a cluster of eight to fifteen villages called a pir. Cases unresolved by the Munda are escalated to the Manki.
● Mankis and Mundas traditionally had no responsibility for revenue collection or land administration, as the system functioned solely as an internal mechanism of self-rule.
● In 1833, Captain **Thomas Wilkinson**, the Political Agent for Kolhan, codified the Manki–Munda system through a set of thirty-one rules, known as **Wilkinson’s Rules**. This was the first formal recognition of the governance practices of the Ho community.
● The rules introduced new concepts such as **private property** and the designation of community members as **raiyats (tenants)**. These changes weakened communal ownership traditions and facilitated the entry of **dikkus (outsiders)** into Kolhan.
● After 1947, the **Kolhan Government Estate** was dissolved, but Wilkinson’s Rules continued to be applied.
● In **Mora Ho vs. State of Bihar (2000)**, the Patna High Court ruled that Wilkinson s Rules were not statutory laws but customs.
● The court allowed them to remain in operation due to the absence of alternatives. In 2021, the Jharkhand government recognised the **Nyaya Panch**, a traditional judicial system, to assist with revenue-related activities and dispute resolution in tribal areas. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Adivasis of the Ho tribe in Jharkhand protested against the Deputy Commissioner for allegedly undermining their traditional self-governance system by removing village heads.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to Notifiable Diseases in India,
consider the following statements:
I.  	 The power to declare a disease as notifiable
rests solely with the Central Government.
II. 	 A notifiable disease is required by law to be
reported to government health authorities.
III. 	Cancer is declared a notifiable disease across
India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In India, the **State Governments have the authority to declare a disease as notifiable** within their jurisdiction. While the Central Government may recommend certain diseases for national surveillance, the responsibility of declaration lies with the states. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● A notifiable disease must be **reported by law to government health authorities.** Notification helps in surveillance, outbreak detection, and control measures. Medical practitioners and diagnostic labs are **legally bound to report cases** of these diseases to the local health department. Examples include cholera, tuberculosis, dengue, AIDS, leprosy, and measles. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Cancer is **not yet declared a notifiable disease** across India, though some states (like Kerala, Himachal Pradesh, and Mizoram) have made it notifiable to improve reporting and management. Recently, a Parliamentary Committee has recommended that cancer should be included in the national list of notifiable diseases, but this has not yet been implemented countrywide. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance: The** Parliamentary Standing Committee on Health and Family Welfare in 2022 recommended the classification of cancer as a notifiable disease.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
With reference to Tea in India, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It is classified as an essential commodity
under the Essential Commodities Act, 1955.
II. 	 Unprocessed green tea leaves are exempted
from Goods and Services Tax (GST).
III. 	Assam contributes over 50% of India’s total
tea production.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India is the **second-largest producer and consumer of tea in the world** after China. Tea is not just a commercial crop but also an important part of India’s culture and exports.
● **Tea is not classified as an essential commodity** under the Essential Commodities Act 1955 because tea is not considered a "foodstuff" under the Act. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● Under India’s GST regime, **unprocessed green tea leaves are exempted from GST** (0%). However, once processed into packaged tea, it attracts GST. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The total area under tea cultivation in Assam accounts for more than half of the country’s total area under tea. **Assam alone produces more than half of India’s tea production.** The estimated annual average production of tea in Assam is about 630- 700 million kg. It is followed by **West Bengal,** and southern states like **Tamil Nadu and Kerala**. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:**  The cost price issue of green tea leaves continues to haunt small growers.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Unique Disability ID
(UDID) Card, consider the following statements.
I. 	 It serves as a single, nationwide identification
document for persons with disabilities.
II. 	 It can be issued by any hospital where
the person with disability is undergoing treatment.
III. 	Different colour codes on the card indicate
the degree and category of disability.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● “**Unique ID for Persons with Disabilities**” project is being implemented with a view to creating a National Database for PwDs, and to issue a Unique Disability Identity Card **(UDID)** to each person with disabilities. UDID card is a **single document of Identification for Persons with Disabilities**. It is the **universal ID that is accepted all across the country.** **So, statement I is correct.**
● As per the new process, a Person with Disability visits the hospital, gets assessed by the concerned specialist, and is issued a **Disability certificate and ID card at the District hospital or the hospital** **where one is currently undergoing treatment. So, statement II is correct.**
● Three types of coloured based UDID Cards are issued based on the severity of the disability. **So, statement** **III is correct.**
○ **White Card:** When the disability percentage of a PwD is below 40% issued. ○ **Yellow Card:** When the disability percentage of a PwD is above 40% but up to 79%. ○ **Blue Card**: When the disability percentage of a PwD is 80% and above. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** As per the recent data, less than 40% of India’s projected population of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) have been issued a Unique Disability ID (UDID) Card.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Major Species (Keystone, Indicator, Invasive, Endemic, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Recently, the Giant African Snail posed serious
risks for which one of the following reasons?

options.a: It negatively impacts animals like salamanders.
options.b:
It acts as a carrier of parasites such as the rat
lungworm.
options.c: It is a predator of beneficial insects.
options.d: It releases toxic substances into the soil.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Giant African Snail (Lissachatina fulica)** is listed among the **world’s worst invasive alien species.** Native to East Africa, it has spread to many tropical and subtropical regions, including India, due to trade and accidental introduction. It thrives in **warm, humid environments** and feeds on over 500 varieties of crops and plants, **threatening agriculture, biodiversity, and human health.**
● The Giant African Snail is a **vector for parasitic nematodes**, especially **Angiostrongylus cantonensis** **(rat lungworm)** and **A. costaricensis**. These parasites can cause **eosinophilic meningitis (brain** **infection)** and **abdominal angiostrongyliasis** **in humans.** Infection generally occurs through ingestion of contaminated snails or their mucus residues on unwashed fruits and vegetables. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, experts warned that the Giant African Snail’s (Lissachatina fulica) presence poses serious risks to Chennai city residents.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Wildlife Conservation (Tiger, Elephant, Lion, Rhino, Crocodile, Vulture, Tortoise, Dolphin, etc.)
questions.questionText:
It is Africa’s most threatened great ape, with only
around 200 to 300 individuals left in the wild. It has earned the iconic “silverback” designation for silver patches on male backs. Which is this species?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Cross River gorilla** (Gorilla gorilla diehli) is a **critically endangered subspecies** of the Western Gorilla.
● It is considered **Africa’s rarest great ape**. It inhabits remote mountain forests along the **Nigeria–** **Cameroon border**, making encounters extremely rare.
● It lives in the mountainous forests of the **Cross River basin**, the **Afi Mountains**, the **Mbe Mountains**, and the **Takamanda National Park.**
● Their habitats include dense rainforests and bamboo groves, which provide natural seclusion from human activity.
● It is **critically endangered**, with an estimated population of only **200 to 300** individuals remaining in the wild. The average lifespan of these gorillas ranges between **35 and 50 years.**
● They typically live at elevations ranging from 1,500 to 3,500 meters above sea level, which adds to the difficulty of observing them in the wild.
● It has fur that varies from **brownish-grey to black**. Its face, hands, and feet are hairless. A distinctive red crest appears on its **cone-shaped head**, and adult males develop silver patches on their backs, giving them the well-known **“silverback” designation**. These physical traits make the Cross River gorilla different from other gorilla subspecies. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Cross River gorilla, Africa’s rarest great ape, survives in the remote Nigeria–Cameroon forests but faces critical endangerment requiring urgent conservation efforts.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
With reference to Swachh Vayu Survekshan
(SVS), consider the following statements :
I. - It categorizes cities into three groups based on population.
II.	 It is not based on the measurement of the air
quality parameters to rank the cities.
III. 	Amravati in Maharashtra is the cleanest city
in India according to the 2025 survey.
Which of the statements given above are **not** correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Swachh Vayu Survekshan (SVS)** is a central initiative to rank cities on the basis of the implementation of approved activities to improve air quality in **130 cities.** 131 cities are categorized into **three groups** **based on population.** 47 cities are in the first group, having a population of more than 10 lakh. 44 cities are in the second group, having a population between 3 to 10 lakh. The third group consists of 40 cities having a population of less than 3 lakh.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● It ranks cities based on the **implementation of air pollution mitigation measures** under the **National** **Clean Air Programme (NCAP)**. The survey assigns **low weightage to the reduction in PM10** **concentration levels**, focusing more on actions taken. Hence, some cities with **higher PM10 levels still** **ranked at the top**.  **So, statement II is correct.**
● In **2025**, **Indore** topped the list of million-plus population cities despite reporting a slight **increase in** **PM10 levels** compared to 2017-18. **So, statement III is not correct.**
○ **Amravati** topped in the **3–10 lakh population category**, while **Dewas** led among **cities with a** **population under 3 lakh**. ○ **Other top cities** in the million-plus category include **Jabalpur (2nd), Agra and Surat (joint** **3rd), Navi Mumbai (4th), and Kanpur (5th)**. Delhi ranked **32nd**, while **Chennai stood at 41st,** but was the **only metro meeting air quality standards** (PM10: 58 μg/m³). **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Key Facts About Swachh Vayu Survekshan (SVS):**
● The programme “Swachh Vayu Survekshan” is covered under the sub-component-3 “National and State level interventions and public outreach activities” of the component **"National Mission for Clean Air" (NMCA)** of the central sector scheme "Control of Pollution".
● Cities are assessed based on their self-assessment report submitted to the Central Pollution Control Board on various parameters, such as biomass and municipal solid waste burning, dust on the roads, dust from construction and demolition waste, vehicular emissions, emissions from industries, creating awareness among the public, and improvement in PM10 concentrations.
● The **objectives** of Swachh Vayu Survekshan are: ○ To create awareness among all sections of society ○ Inform citizens about the health impacts related to exposure ○ Comparing air quality conditions at different locations/cities ○ To achieve the goal of NCAP “Clean air for all”.
**Relevance:** Indore, the cleanest city in India, has also topped the list of million-plus population cities in the Swachh Vayu Survekshan survey 2025.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
The SPREE-2025 and the AMNESTY-2025
initiatives are associated with:

options.a:
Expanding social security coverage for
workers
options.b:
Expanding financial inclusion through labour
reforms
options.c:
Encouraging formal sector employment
through skill certification
options.d:
Strengthening public distribution and food
security systems

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC)** has launched two major initiatives – **SPREE-** **2025 (Scheme for Promoting Registration of Employers and Employees)** and the **AMNESTY** **Scheme-2025**.
● These aim to **expand social security coverage**, simplify compliance for industries, and provide employees with **enhanced health and welfare benefits**.
● SPREE-2025 will remain operational until **31st December 2025**. Its primary objective is to encourage voluntary registration of industries and employees under ESIC without relying on punitive measures.
● Under this scheme, employers and employees who are not yet associated with ESIC can register through the **ESI Portal, the Shram Suvidha Portal, and the Company Affairs Portal**.
● The Amnesty Scheme 2025 is a one-time dispute resolution initiative aimed at reducing the backlog of court cases and **promoting compliance under the ESI Act**.
○ The scheme will come into effect on 1st October 2025 and remain valid until 30th September 2026, providing a **structured mechanism for employers to resolve legal disputes** with ESIC through out-of-court settlements. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Key Facts About Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC):**
● It is a statutory corporate body set up under the ESI Act 1948, which is responsible for the administration of the ESI Scheme.
● The **Employees’ State Insurance Act, 1948,** was the first major legislation on **social** **security for workers** in independent India.
● It was enacted at a time when industries were still developing, and manpower deployment in manufacturing was limited to a few sectors like **jute, textiles, and** **chemicals**.
● The scheme was formally **inaugurated in Kanpur on 24th February 1952** (now observed as **ESIC Day**) by **Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru**.
● The Act aimed to create a **multi-dimensional social security system** despite India’s fledgling economy, making it a remarkable step for **socio-economic welfare**.
● The ESI Act, 1948, covers workers against health-related contingencies such as: ○ Sickness ○ Maternity ○ Temporary or permanent disablement ○ Occupational diseases ○ Death due to employment injury
**Relevance:** The Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) has launched two major initiatives — SPREE2025 and the AMNESTY Scheme-2025.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Food processing and preservation
II. 	 Soil fertility improvement
III. 	Penicillin production
How many of the above are applications of Aspergillus niger?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Aspergillus niger** is a species of filamentous fungi belonging to the genus Aspergillus, commonly known as **black** **aspergillus**.
● The genus Aspergillus comprises a diverse group of filamentous fungi that are ubiquitously distributed across various ecological niches and are of considerable medical, industrial, and ecological significance.
● A. niger is a crucial organism in **fermentation processes** for producing enzymes, making it valuable in biotechnology and **food industries in processing and preservation. So, point I is correct.**
● A. niger is a spore-forming fungal biofertilizer used to enhance nutrient availability, plant vigor, and **composting efficiency in soil systems.** Known for its powerful organic acid production and enzymatic activity, A. niger solubilizes phosphorus, potassium, and key micronutrients, while breaking down organic matter to **improve soil texture and fertility.** This biostimulant supports early root development, enhances plant tolerance to abiotic stress, and accelerates compost maturation, making it ideal for sustainable, organic, and regenerative farming systems. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Penicillin** is derived from species of the fungus Penicillium, most notably Penicillium chrysogenum and Penicillium rubens, and **not from Aspergillus niger.  So, point III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recent research from the Western Ghats by Indian scientists has identified new related species (e.g., A. dhakephalkarii, A. patriciawiltshireae), underscoring the biodiversity and industrial relevance of this group.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
The All India Debt & Investment Survey (AIDIS)
is conducted by:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The National Statistical Office (NSO) conducts All India Debt & Investment Survey (AIDIS)** in the rural and urban areas of the country at periodic intervals. The survey provides critical data on household indebtedness and asset ownership across both rural and urban areas. Its findings are instrumental in shaping national accounts, assessing inequality in asset distribution, understanding credit markets, and informing policies of the RBI, MoSPI, and other government institutions. **So, option c is correct.**
● The National Statistics Office (NSO) is an attached office under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI). **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The All India Debt and Investment Survey (AIDIS) is scheduled to be conducted from July 2026 to June 2027.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Financial Markets (Money Market, Capital Market, SEBI)
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following statements most
appropriately describes a ‘Perpetual Bond’?

options.a:
A debt instrument whose interest rate is
adjusted periodically according to market conditions
options.b:
A bond that provides investors with an option
to convert the debt into equity shares after a specific period
options.c:
A debt instrument that allows the bondholder
to sell the security to the issuer even before maturity
options.d:
A fixed-income debt security that does not
have any maturity date and pays interest indefinitely

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **A perpetual bond is a fixed-income security that has no maturity date and theoretically pays interest** **forever.** Instead of returning principal on a set schedule, the issuer pays interest indefinitely.
○ These bonds have an obligation only to pay interest and are not required to repay the debt. The perpetual bond means the issuer is under no obligation to redeem the principal amount at any point. ○ Investors of these bonds **do not get their principal back**. However, the interest continues forever. **So, option d is correct.**
● Floating rate bonds, also known as floaters or variable rate bonds, are fixed income instruments whose coupon (interest) rates get changed at periodic intervals. **So, option a is not correct.**
● A convertible bond is a hybrid security that offers interest payments while providing the option for conversion into a set number of the issuing company’s equity shares. **So, option b is not correct.**
● A Puttable Bond is a debt instrument that allows the bondholder to force the issuer to repurchase the security at specified dates before maturity. **So, option c is not correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency Ltd (IREDA) recently raised ₹453 crore at 7.70% per annum through its second issue of Perpetual Bonds.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Isobutanol is an alcoholic compound that has
applications in which of the following?
I. 	 Biofuel for vehicles
II. 	 Industrial solvent
III. 	Hydrating agent for the eyes
IV. 	 Industrial fire extinguisher
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Isobutanol is an alcohol compound with flammable properties. Isobutanol, also called isobutyl alcohol, is an alcohol with the chemical formula C₄H₁₀O and one of the four isomers of butanol. It is a clear, colorless liquid with a characteristic odor.  It is only moderately soluble in water.
● **Isobutanol can be used as a biofuel** because, like ethanol, it can be manufactured from plants. It can be made from ethanol using fermentation processes. It possesses some favorable properties that make it an attractive fuel for internal combustion engines.  For instance, when compared to ethanol, isobutanol features a higher heating value. Isobutanol is less corrosive than ethanol and is much less hygroscopic,
which enables it to be transported using the existing fuel infrastructure. **So, pointoption I is correct.**
● It is commonly used **as a solvent in varied industries, including paints and coatings.** It is used as a solvent in the flavor, fragrance, pharmaceutical, and pesticide industries and as a **chemical** **manufacturing ingredient for products such as lacquer, paint strippers, paint primer, and craft** **paints**. **So, pointoption II is correct.**
○ It is used as an additive in polishes and cleaners, e. g. floor cleaners and stain removers. ○ Isobutanol can be used to extract drugs and natural substances such as antibiotics, hormones, vitamins, alkaloids, and camphor from a fermentation broth or an aqueous solution, or a solid substance. ○ In the pesticide industry, isobutanol is mainly used in the synthesis of the intermediate of diazine phosphorus, namely isobutyronitrile.
● Skin contact, ingestion, and inhalation of isobutanol can be harmful to health. The compound causes skin irritation and **severe eye damage, including loss of vision**. **So, pointoption III is not correct.**
● Isobutanol is an alcoholic compound with flammable properties. It is **very flammable** and has a flash point that is only slightly above normal room temperatures. **So, pointoption IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Union Minister for Transport Nitin Gadkari said recently that the Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI) is working to explore the possibility of blending 10% isobutanol with diesel.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Ramsar Sites, World Heritage Sites, UNESCO Heritage Sites, WHS, Biodiversity Heritage Sites, etc.
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Biodiversity Heritage Sites
(BHS) in India, consider the following statements:
I. 	 These may include areas rich in both
wild and domesticated species with wild, domesticated, or cultivated species diversity.
II. 	 They can be notified solely by the Central
Government under the Biological Diversity Act, 2002.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Biodiversity Heritage Sites (BHS) are areas of ecological significance, **rich in both wild and** **domesticated species**, rare and threatened species, and areas with high endemism or evolutionary significance.
○ **These are the areas that are unique, ecologically fragile ecosystems having rich biodiversity** **comprising of any one or more of the components such as**; species richness, high endemism, presence of rare, endemic and threatened species, keystone species, species of evolutionary significance, **wild ancestors of domestic/cultivated species** or land races or their varieties, past pre-eminence of biological components represented by fossil beds and having cultural or aesthetic values. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Biodiversity Heritage Sites (BHS) in India are declared under **Section 37 of the Biological Diversity Act** **of 2002.**
● Under Section 37 of the Biological Diversity Act of 2002, **the State Governments are empowered to** **notify areas of biodiversity importance as Biodiversity Heritage Sites on the recommendations of** **the State Biodiversity Board**. The State Biodiversity Board shall consult the local body and the Biodiversity Management Committee concerned before making such recommendations. **So, statement II** **is not correct.**
○ State Biodiversity Boards (SBB) may invite suggestions (or consider those already coming from communities) for declaration of BHSs, through the Biodiversity Management Committees (BMCs) and other relevant community institutions. ○ Under subsection (2) of Section 37 of the BD Act, the State Government in consultation with the Central Government, may frame rules for the management and conservation of BHS. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● Criteria for Identification of BHS: **Areas having any of the following characteristics** **may qualify for inclusion as BHS-**
○ Areas that contain a mosaic of natural, semi-natural, and manmade habitats,
which together contain a significant diversity of life forms. ○ Areas that contain significant domesticated biodiversity components and/or representative agro-ecosystems with ongoing agricultural practices that sustain this diversity. ○  Areas that are significant from a biodiversity point of view as also important cultural spaces, such as sacred groves/trees and sites, or other large community conserved areas. ○ Areas, including very small ones, that offer refuge or corridors for threatened and endemic fauna and flora, such as community conserved areas or urban greens and wetlands. ○ Areas that provide habitats, aquatic or terrestrial, for seasonal migrant species for feeding and breeding. ○ Areas that are maintained as preservation plots by the research wing of the Forestof Forest Department. ○ Medicinal Plant Conservation Areas.
**Relevance:** Recently, the government declared 8.6 acres of green cover at the Cantonment Railway Colony in Bengaluru as a Biodiversity Heritage Site — the city’s second after GKVK.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Initiative Related to
I. - New York Declaration 2025
Russia-Ukraine conflict
II. - New Delhi Declaration 2025
Preservation of manuscripts
III. - Kashi Declaration
Women empowerment
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The United Nations General Assembly recently endorsed the **New York Declaration on a two-state** **solution between Israel and Palestine.** The New York Declaration is the outcome of an international conference held in July 2025 at UN Headquarters, organized by France and Saudi Arabia.
○ The New York Declaration “**lays out a single roadmap to deliver the two-State solution”.** This involves an immediate ceasefire in Gaza, the release of all hostages held there, and the establishment of a Palestinian State that is both viable and sovereign. The roadmap further calls for the disarmament of Hamas and its exclusion from governance in Gaza, normalization between Israel and the Arab countries, as well as collective security guarantees. **So, pair I is not correctly** **matched.**
● **The New Delhi Declaration was adopted to preserve, disseminate ‘vast treasure of manuscripts.** ○ The New Delhi Declaration resolved that manuscripts are “the living memory of a nation, and the foundation of its civilisational identity”. ○ The declaration also resolved to “acquire and repatriate original manuscripts or secure their digital copies”. ○ The Declaration was adopted on the closing day of the three-day 'Gyan Bharatam' conference on India's manuscript heritage, hosted at the Vigyan Bhawan in New Delhi. **So, pair II is correctly** **matched.**
● Recently, the Youth Spiritual Summit culminated in Varanasi with the launch of **the Kashi Declaration.** **Kashi Declaration Sets 5-Year  Roadmap For Youth-Led Drug De-Addiction Movement.** The resolution expressed commitment to a nationwide campaign for drug-free youth. **So, pair III is not** **correctly matched.**
○ The Youth Spiritual Summit was conducted on the theme Nasha Mukt Yuva for Viksit Bharat. It was organised by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. ○ The **Kashi Declaration affirms a national consensus to treat substance abuse as a** **multifaceted public health and societal challenge**. It calls for a whole-of-government and wholeof-society approach to prevent addiction, support recovery, and foster a national culture of sobriety. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** General Assembly endorses the New York Declaration on the two-state solution between Israel and Palestine

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Flamingo and Iskander-K, recently seen in the
news, are:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Flamingo and Iskander-K** are **missile systems** reported in the context of global military developments.
● The **Flamingo FP-5** is a newly developed Ukrainian **long-range cruise missile**. It is capable of flying **up** **to 3,000 km** and carrying a **massive 1,150 kg warhead**, making it a serious deterrent against Russian targets across European Russia.
● The **Iskander-K** is a Russian **mobile short-range cruise missile system**, mounted on an **8x8 military** **truck chassis**. Entering service in **2009**, it can be fitted with two to six container launchers firing missiles such as the **9M728 (R-500)** or **9M729**, which are land versions of Russia’s **Kalibr** cruise missiles. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Hellfire Missile**
● It is an air-to-ground, laser-guided, subsonic tactical missile with significant anti-tank capacity.
● It is used by the United States military as well as 30 US allies. **Zircon Missile**
● The 3M22 Zircon (Tsirkon), NATO code-named SS-N-33, is a scramjet-powered hypersonic cruise missile developed by Russia.
● One of the key features of the Zircon is its ability to generate a plasma cloud during hypersonic flight, which absorbs radio waves and makes the missile more difficult to detect by radar. This phenomenon is known as plasma stealth.
**Relevance:** Flamingo, the missile that brings European Russia within Kyiv’s range

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Foreigners Tribunal:
I. 	 It is established under the Citizenship
(Amendment) Act of 2019.
II. 	 It can be set up only by the Central
Government.
III. 	It has been granted the powers of a Judicial
Magistrate First Class under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Foreigners Tribunals (FTs)** are **quasi-judicial bodies** constituted under the **Foreigners (Tribunals)** **Order, 1964,** issued under Section 3 of the **Foreigners Act, 1946**. They allow state authorities to refer cases of individuals suspected of being foreigners. **So, statement I is not correct.**
○ Although the 1964 Order extends to the entire country, **FTs currently operate only in Assam.** In other states, suspected illegal immigrants are produced before local courts and dealt with under the provisions of the Foreigners Act, 1946.
● Originally, the power to set up FTs rested solely with the **Central Government.** However, the 2019 amendment to the Foreigners (Tribunals) Order empowered **State Governments** as well to establish such tribunals. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The Immigration and Foreigners Order, 2025, issued under the newly enacted **Immigration and** **Foreigners Act, 2025**, replaces the earlier **Foreigners (Tribunal) Order, 1964**. It authorises **Foreigners** **Tribunals (FTs)** to issue arrest warrants and detain individuals unable to prove their Indian nationality, powers that were earlier exercised through executive orders.
● Under the Order, FTs may issue arrest warrants against persons failing to appear when their nationality is in dispute. Furthermore, FTs are vested with the **powers of a civil court** under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, and the authority of a **Judicial Magistrate First Class** under the **Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha** **Sanhita****, 2023****. So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Union Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has vested Foreigners Tribunals (FTs) with the powers of a Judicial Magistrate First Class.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText: What is Pink Tax?

options.a:
A tax on cosmetic and beauty products
marketed for women
options.b:
An indirect tax collected by the government
to fund women-oriented welfare schemes
options.c:
Higher prices for products and services
marketed towards women than similar products for men
options.d:
A financial penalty imposed on companies
that fail to ensure gender-neutral pricing

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Pink Tax** refers to the **higher prices that women end up paying for comparable goods and services** **simply because they are branded or marketed for women.** Pink tax is neither a real tax nor a government-imposed fee.  Examples include more expensive women’s shampoos, clothing, or even salon services like haircuts. Studies from the U.S. and U.K. have shown that women’s products are often significantly costlier than men’s, despite being nearly identical in quality. This pricing disparity increases women’s financial burden over their lifetimes. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** As per a study done in the U.S., personal care products targeting women were 13% costlier than men.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Erra Matti Dibbalu, a notified National
Geo-heritage Monument in Andhra Pradesh that was in the news recently, is known for which one of the following geological features?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Erra Matti Dibbalu,** also known as the **Red Sand Dunes**, is a notified National Geo-heritage Monument located along the coast near Visakhapatnam.
● Spread over 1,500 acres, the formations are composed of **sand, silt, and clay, with their distinct reddish** **hue** resulting from natural oxidation over thousands of years.
● The site contains **dendritic drainage patterns** and sediment layers that record fluctuations in sea level and climatic changes over the **late Quaternary period.**
● First documented by British geologist **William King in 1886**, these formations are regarded as rare coastal geomorphological features.
● **Andhra Pradesh’s** Erra Matti Dibbalu and the Natural Heritage of the Tirumala Hills, including the Eparchaean Unconformity, have been included in the **UNESCO Tentative List of World Heritage Sites.**
● Only two other similar sites exist in the world, one in **Sri Lanka and another in Tamil Nadu.**
● The Geological Survey of India (GSI) declared Erra Matti Dibbalu a **National Geo-heritage Monument** in 2016. However, experts note that the site faces threats from tourism and filming activities, highlighting the need for stronger conservation measures. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Andhra Pradesh’s Erra Matti Dibbalu and the Natural Heritage of the Tirumala Hills have been included in the UNESCO Tentative List of World Heritage Sites.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Missile Technology
Control Regime (MTCR), consider the following statements:
I. 	 It imposes export controls on technical
components and software related to missile production.
II. 	 India is one of the founding members of the
MTCR.
III. 	It has no legally binding provisions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
