● The **Missile Technology Control Regime (MTCR)** is a voluntary, intergovernmental export control arrangement established in **1987** by seven countries — the U.S., U.K., France, Germany, Italy, Japan, and Canada — to prevent the proliferation of **missiles and unmanned delivery systems capable of carrying** **nuclear, chemical, or biological weapons**.
● MTCR guidelines clearly state that its members agree to place export restrictions on missile systems (both rockets and UAVs) as well as **technical components and software** that can be used in missile production. The idea is to prevent technology transfer that could enable countries to develop delivery systems for WMDs (Weapons of Mass Destruction). **So, statement I is correct.**
● India joined the MTCR only in **2016**, becoming the 35th member. The founding members in 1987 were **Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States**. **So, statement** **II is not correct.**
● The MTCR is not a treaty but a **voluntary arrangement**. It has **no legally binding provisions** and no central enforcement mechanism. Compliance depends on member states’ willingness to implement export controls. If disputes arise, they are settled bilaterally or discussed at annual plenary meetings. **So,** **statement III is correct.**
● The MTCR maintains a **two-tier control list**: ○ **Category I:** Most sensitive, including complete missile systems capable of carrying 500 kg payloads to 300 km or more, and their major subsystems. These transfers are strictly restricted.
○ **Category II:** Less sensitive items, including propulsion, launch technology, and missile systems with a range of at least 300 kilometers, no matter the payload**.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The U.S. is expected to unilaterally reinterpret the MTCR agreement to sell sophisticated “Reaper” style and other advanced military drones abroad.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
marsupials:
I. 	 Some marsupials are found in tropical
rainforests.
II. 	 All marsupials have pouches.
III. 	No marsupial species is naturally found in
India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Marsupials (Metatheria) are pouched mammals, that is, mammals in which the females bear their young alive but in an almost foetal state and, in most species, carry them within an external pocket or pouch formed by a flap of dermal tissue on the abdomen. Generally, **no, male marsupials don’t have pouches.** **Only females have them** because they are the ones protecting the young. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● Marsupials reside in a wide range of habitats. They can be found in dry scrublands, **temperate deciduous** **forests, and tropical rainforests.** Marsupials are found in the different regions of North, South, and Central America, Australia, New Zealand, and Tasmania. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The Marsupials are native to Australia. **They are not naturally found in India.** They are mostly found in arid and semi-arid regions, and not only in montane grasslands with no predators. **So, statement III is** **correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:**  Recently, Australia approved the world's first vaccine to save koalas from chlamydia.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
In the context of Indian aviation, what is damp
leasing?

options.a:
The lessor supplies the aircraft only; the lessee
provides crew, maintenance, and insurance.
options.b:
The lessor provides the aircraft, pilots,  and
maintenance, while the lessee supplies the cabin crew.
options.c:
The lessor provides the aircraft and full crew,
while the lessee manages ground operations and scheduling.
options.d:
The lessor provides the aircraft and
maintenance, while the lessee provides both flight and full crew.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Damp Leasing is a leasing agreement under which an **airline company, the lessor, provides the aircraft** **along with flight crew (pilots) and maintenance, while the lessee provides the cabin crew.**
○ Wet leasing means renting the plane along with the operating crew and engineers, while dry leasing refers to taking only the aircraft on rent**.**
● The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) rules allow airlines to wet or damp lease foreignregistered aircraft for a period of 12 months, which is extendable once by a further six months. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) grants IndiGo a one-time final extension to operate Turkish Airlines aircraft on damp lease.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Acanthamoeba was recently seen in the news in
one of the following contexts?

options.d:
A viral pathogen responsible for Japanese
encephalitis

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Acanthamoeba is a **free-living amoeba** commonly found in soil, water, and air. It was recently in the news in India, especially Kerala, due to reports of **amoebic meningoencephalitis and keratitis** associated with contaminated well water and ponds.
● Acanthamoeba is capable of causing **life-threatening brain infections**, medically known as **granulomatous amoebic meningoencephalitis (GAE)**. Recent cases in Kerala, linked to exposure to contaminated well water, have made it a matter of public health concern. Although GAE is rare, it is often **fatal in immunocompromised individuals**. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, it has been revealed that Acanthamoeba is more widespread in Kerala s waterbodies than was thought earlier.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Critical and Strategic Minerals (Lithium, Cobalt, Titanium, KABIL, REEs, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the mining of critical minerals
in India, consider the following statements:
I. 	 All projects are reviewed by the Central
Government regardless of the mining lease area.
II. 	 The captive mines are not allowed to sell
accumulated mineral deposits.
III.	 It is mandatory to have public consultations
for all mining projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Critical minerals** are those minerals that are essential for economic development and national security.
● The Union **Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)** has notified that in accordance with the provisions of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA), 2006 and keeping in view the requirement of national defence, security and strategic considerations, all mining projects involving atomic minerals notified in Part B of the First Schedule of the Minerals and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act and **critical and strategic minerals notified in Part D** are exempted from public hearing.
○ The order also clarified that these **projects will be reviewed at the central level,** irrespective of how large or small the mining lease area is. **So, statement I is correct.** ○ The notification exempts mining proposals of 30 atomic, critical, and strategic minerals, including tungsten, from the ambit of **public consultations. So, statement III is not correct.**
● Current regulations **prohibit captive mines from selling these accumulated deposits**. The proposed regulatory changes would authorise states to allow the sale of stockpiles within leased areas upon payment of supplementary fees. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **Environmental Impact Assessment or Environmental Management Plan report** of these mining projects will have to include details of impact on local settlements/population, social infrastructure (such as medical facilities, drinking water facilities), skill development and employment opportunities, public grievance redressal system, etc., among other aspects. This will be done so that the concerned regional expert appraisal committee can comprehensively review the proposal and prepare an appropriate environmental management plan. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Key Facts About Critical Minerals:**
● Countries identify minerals critical for them based on their national priorities. In 2023, the Ministry of Mines released a list of **30 critical minerals for India.**
○ These minerals are Antimony, Beryllium, Bismuth, Cobalt, Copper, Gallium, Germanium, Graphite, Hafnium, Indium, Lithium, Molybdenum, Niobium, Nickel, PGE, phosphorus, Potash, REE, Rhenium, Silicon, Strontium, Tantalum, Tellurium, Tin, Titanium, Tungsten, Vanadium, Zirconium, Selenium, and Cadmium.
● Critical minerals are essential for clean energy technologies like solar panels, wind turbines, EVs, and energy storage systems. To secure these resources, India launched the **National Critical** **Mineral Mission (NCMM) in 2025** to ensure their long-term availability and processing.
○ Under this mission, the **Geological Survey of India (GSI)** has been tasked with conducting 1,200 exploration projects from 2024-25 to 2030-31.
● A committee formed by the Ministry of Mines in November 2022 identified 30 critical minerals, with 24 included in Part D of Schedule I of the Mines and Minerals Development and Regulation Act, 1957 (MMDR Act, 1957). The inclusion of 24 critical minerals in Part D of the First Schedule of the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act (MMDR Act) means that the Central Government now has the exclusive authority to auction mining leases and composite licenses for these specific minerals.
● It also recommended setting up a **Centre of Excellence on Critical Minerals (CECM)** to regularly update the mineral list and guide strategy.
**Relevance:** Opposition leaders in Tamil Nadu condemned the Union government's decision to exempt critical mineral mining from public consultation.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
Which of the following are celebrated as harvest
festivals in India?
I. 	 Karam Parab
II. 	 Langkhun Festival
III. 	Bisu Parba
IV. 	 Hemis Festival
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Karam Parab**, also called **Karma Puja**, is one of the most significant tribal harvest festivals. It is celebrated mainly in **Jharkhand, Odisha, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, West Bengal, and** **Assam**. Communities such as the **Munda, Ho, Oraon, Baiga, Kharia, and Santhal** people participate. The festival centres around worship of the **Karam tree**, symbolising fertility and prosperity. **So, point I** **is correct.**
● The **Langkhun festival** is a socio-religious event observed by the **Tiwa community** (also known as **Lalung**) in Assam. It is particularly celebrated in the **Karbi Anglong district**. The festival is held with the purpose of praying for a good **Rabi crop harvest**. Its significance lies in maintaining agricultural prosperity and community solidarity. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Bisu Parba** is the new year and **harvest festival of the Tulu region of India.** It marks the first day of Paggu, the first month of the Tulu calendar, also called Thingade/Singade. Generally, it falls in the middle of April as per the Gregorian calendar. **So, point III is correct.**
● The **Hemis Festival** is a grand, Buddhist celebration in Ladakh, held at Hemis Monastery. The two-day event is one of the most important festivals for Buddhists and is celebrated to mark the birth anniversary of Guru Padmasambhava or Guru Rimpoche, who is regarded as an incarnation of Lord Buddha, and is also credited for having brought Buddhism to Ladakh and Tibet. **So, point IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Karam Parab was celebrated recently.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Reports and Indices (HDI, CPI, GHI, WEO, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Global Peace Index is released by which one of
the following organisations?

options.a:
Wellbeing Research Centre at the University
of Oxford

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Global Peace Index** is an annual ranking compiled by the **Institute for Economics and Peace (IEP)**.
● This list has covered 163 independent states and territories and represents 99.7% of the global population.
● The index is responsible for assessing peace across three parameters**-societal security and safety,** **ongoing domestic or international disputes, and militarisation**. Iceland has achieved the highest rankings all over three areas in 2025.
● India has been awarded 115th rank in the Global Peace Index, 2025, showing a modest change from its earlier position.
● Following Iceland in the global peace rankings are Ireland, New Zealand, Finland, Austria, Switzerland, Singapore, Portugal, Denmark, and Slovenia. These countries’ names have been declared as the top ten most peaceful nations in the world. Europe, on broader terms, continues to cover the upper slot of peaceful countries. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Iceland has once again secured its position as the most peaceful country in the world, as per the 2025 Global Peace Index (GPI).

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Tiger Reserve, Elephant Reserve, other species-specific reserves, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following protected areas:
I. 	 Debrigarh Wildlife Sanctuary
II. 	 Gandhi Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary
III. 	Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
How many of the above are Tiger Reserves?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Debrigarh Wildlife Sanctuary:** It is situated in **Odisha**. It was declared a **wildlife sanctuary in 1985.** It finds a special mention because of noted freedom fighter **Veer Surendra Sai**.  During his rebellion against the British, his base at Barapathara was located within the sanctuary. Most of the vegetation in the sanctuary is covered with a mixed and dry deciduous forest. **Recently, it has been declared a Tiger** **reserve. So, point I is correct.**
● **Gandhi Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary:** It is situated in northwestern **Madhya Pradesh**, along the Madhya Pradesh-Rajasthan border, and lies within the **Khathiar-Gir dry deciduous forests** ecoregion. The Chambal River flows through the sanctuary, dividing it into two parts. It is a designated **Important Bird** **and Biodiversity Area (IBA).**
○ It mainly hosts fauna like Chinkara, Nilgai, and Spotted Deer, Indian Leopard, Striped Hyena, and Jackal. However, **it is not a tiger reserve. So, point II is not correct.**
● **Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary**: A dam was constructed across the Periyar River in 1895. The forest around the new lake was declared a Reserve Forest in 1899. In 1934, it was established as Nellikkampetty Sanctuary. This was extended and renamed Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary in 1950. **It became a Tiger** **Reserve in 1978**, with the core area declared a National Park in 1982. **So, point III is correct.**
○ It hosts Tiger, leopard, elephant, gaur, sambar, wild dog, barking deer, lion-tailed macaque, nilgiri langur, nilgiri tahr, etc. **So, only two of the above protected areas are Tiger Reserves.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Debrigarh Wildlife Sanctuary has been declared a Tiger reserve.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Pre-agricultural level of technology
II. 	 High population growth
III. 	Economic backwardness
IV. 	 Low levels of literacy
Which of the above-mentioned are the criteria used to declare a Scheduled Tribe as a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs)** are a sub-category of Scheduled Tribes (STs), which was created based on the recommendations of the **Dhebar Commission** (1960-61) — led by former Member of Parliament U N Dhebar — which had investigated the various issues faced by STs, and in consultation with state governments.
○ The Commission identified the disparity in socio-economic and living conditions between different tribal groups, and said that some tribal groups were more vulnerable than others.
● **The criteria include:** ○ Preservation of pre-agricultural practices. **So, point I is correct.** ○ Economic Backwardness. **So, point III is correct.** ○ Decreasing or stagnant population growth. **So, point II is not correct.** ○ Relatively low levels of literacy in contrast to other tribal groups. **So, point IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Ministry of Tribal Affairs wrote to the Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India, requesting them to consider enumerating PVTGs separately in the upcoming Census.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
'Hubble Constant' is sometimes seen in the news
in which one of the following contexts?

options.a:
Measuring the rate of expansion of the
Universe
options.b: Determining the density of dark matter
options.c:
Determining the proportion of dark energy in
the cosmos
options.d:
Calculating the age of stars based on their
luminosity

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Hubble constant**, denoted as H0, **describes the rate at which galaxies are moving away from each** **other due to the expansion of the universe.** Recently, in a significant study, the most precise determination of the expansion rate of the universe was made using cool, giant variable stars ‘Mira’ in the galaxy as anchors for the first time.
● The study opens up a new way to **precisely map the age and size of the universe.** Using **‘Mira’ variable** stars enabled the research team to determine the Hubble constant with a remarkable 3.7 per cent precision.
● The **“Hubble tension”** refers to a discrepancy between the measurements of the rate of expansion of the universe, known as the Hubble constant. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge box**
● **Mira**, also known as Omicron Ceti, is a star that **remarkably changes its brightness** **over time, in a regular pattern**. With the variability first measured by astronomers in the 17th century, Mira was the first known example of a "variable star"—a star that doesn't shine with a constant brightness.
● These are a type of giant star that **go through regular cycles of expanding and** **contracting**. **These stars are relatively cool, with surface temperatures around 3,000** **Kelvin** (about half the temperature of the Sun’s surface), and **they are in the late stages** **of their life**. **So, statement III is correct.**
● One of the most important things about Mira variables is that there is a strong relationship between how bright they are and how long their pulsation cycles last. This relationship allows astronomers to use them as **"standard candles”.**
**Relevance:** A new study from IUCAA, with a Nobel laureate as a co-author, used oxygen-rich Mira variable stars to achieve a highly precise measurement of the Hubble constant.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
The Santorini and Kolumbo volcanoes, recently
seen in the news, are located in:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Santorini Volcano:** Located in the **southern Aegean Sea**, about 200 km southeast of mainland **Greece**. It is the largest island of a small archipelago, forming the remnant of a volcanic caldera. Part of the South Aegean Volcanic Arc, and the southernmost member of the Cyclades group. Famous for the Minoan (Thera) eruption (~1600 BCE), one of the largest eruptions in recorded history, which contributed to the collapse of the Minoan civilisation and may have triggered a volcanic winter.
● **Kolumbo Volcano:** A submarine volcano located in the Aegian Sea, about 7 km northeast of Santorini. The last major eruption was in 1650 AD, which caused tsunamis and toxic gas emissions, leading to destruction on Santorini and nearby islands. It forms part of the Christiana-Santorini-Kolumbo (CSK) volcanic field. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, an earthquake swarm of over 1,200 quakes around Santorini revealed that Santorini and the submerged Kolumbo volcano share an underground magma reservoir.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Wildlife Conservation (Tiger, Elephant, Lion, Rhino, Crocodile, Vulture, Tortoise, Dolphin, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following regions has India’s
first Dugong Conservation Reserve?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **India’s first Dugong Conservation Reserve is located in the Palk Bay.** It was established on September 21, 2022, by the Tamil Nadu government under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, It covers 448.34 sq. km. in northern Palk Bay.
● The region is home to over 12,250 hectares of seagrass meadows, vital feeding grounds for dugongs (Dugong dugon).  Seagrasses also support a host of other marine species, making the reserve ecologically significant. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has formally adopted a motion recognising India’s first Dugong Conservation Reserve in Palk Bay.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference
to the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act of 2010:
I. 	 It aims to ensure that foreign donations do
not compromise national sovereignty.
II. 	 It allows NGOs to transfer foreign contributions to other NGOs.
III. 	The annual limit for foreign remittances from
relatives abroad is ₹10 lakh without prior intimation.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA) of 2010** regulates the acceptance and utilisation of foreign funds by individuals, associations, and companies in India. Its primary objective is to ensure that **foreign donations do not compromise national sovereignty**, integrity, or internal security, and are used only for legitimate developmental purposes. **So, statement I is correct.**
● FCRA (Amendment), 2020 - Key Changes: ○ **Prohibits NGOs from transferring foreign contributions to other NGOs or individuals. So,** **statement II is not correct.** ○ All office bearers must provide Aadhaar/passport/OCI details for registration. ○ All contributions must be received only in the designated SBI branch, New Delhi. ○ Reduced administrative expenses  from 50% to 20% of foreign funds. ○ Public servants are prohibited from receiving foreign contributions.
● **FCRA Rules (Amendment), 2022:** ○ Increased the annual limit for foreign remittances from relatives abroad from ₹1 lakh to ₹10 lakh without prior intimation. **So, statement III is correct.**
● **Categories under FCRA** ○ **Prohibited Recipients:** Candidates for elections, journalists, media houses, judges, government servants, members of legislatures, political parties, and organisations of political nature. ○ **Permitted Use:** NGOs, educational, cultural, economic, and social associations, provided they comply with regulations. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Union Home Ministry revoked the FCRA licence of the Students Educational and Cultural Movement of Ladakh (SECMOL), founded by climate activist Sonam Wangchuk.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following statements is correct
about the Agni-Prime Missile?

options.a:
It is a nuclear-capable medium-range cruise
missile
options.b:
It is a two-stage canisterised liquid and solid
propellant ballistic missile
options.c:
It can be launched from a rail-based mobile
launcher system

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Agni-Prime Missile (Agni-P)** is a new generation **nuclear-capable medium-range ballistic missile** **(MRBM).** Agni Prime Ballistic Missile was tested for the first time in June 2021. It is lighter than any of the previous Agni missiles.
● Features of Agni-Prime Missile: It is a **two-stage canisterised solid propellant ballistic missile.** ○ This is a surface-to-surface ballistic missile. ○ Range: 1000 to 2000 km. ○ Payload: Around 1,000 Kg (It can carry high explosive, thermobaric or nuclear warheads). ○ It incorporates upgrades such as propulsion systems, composite rocket motor casings, and advanced navigation and guidance systems. ○ **Agni-Prime may be launched by train or road and stored for an extended period of time.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** DRDO in collaboration with the Strategic Forces Command (SFC), has carried out the successful launch of Intermediate Range Agni-Prime Missile from a Rail based Mobile launcher system.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
The antibacterial drug Ethambutol Hydrochloride,
recently seen in the news, is prescribed for the treatment of:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Ethambutol Hydrochloride** is an antibacterial prescription medicine used for the **treatment of** **tuberculosis (TB).** It is a synthetic, water soluble, heat stable compound.
● It is specifically effective against actively growing microorganisms of the genus Mycobacterium including M. tuberculosis.  It is used to **combat both pulmonary and extrapulmonary tuberculosis.**
● **Mechanism:** Ethambutol HCl specifically targets the synthesis of the cell wall in M. tuberculosis.  By interfering with the polymerization of arabinogalactan, a key component of the mycobacterial cell wall, Ethambutol disrupts the structural integrity of the bacteria, leading to its demise. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India recently launched an anti-dumping investigation on imports of tuberculosis drug Ethambutol Hydrochloride from China and Thailand.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Species Type of
I. - Beddome’s Cat Skink Lizard
II. - Yellow-tailed Ashy Skimmer Dragonfly
III. Pallid Fish Eagle Fish
Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Beddome’s Cat Skink** is also called Beddome's ristella. Beddome’s cat skink is named after British naturalist Richard Henry Beddome, the species is considered rare and endemic to the Western Ghats region. It is a small reddish brown **lizard** with retractile claws and bicarinate scales. It is found in the forests of the **Western Ghats** at an altitude of 400-1,300 metres. It is an oviparous species and egg laying coincides with the southwestern monsoons. **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● **Yellow-tailed Ashy Skimmer** is **a species of dragonfly** in the family Libellulidae. It is one of two species in its group, called Potamarcha. The other species is Potamarcha puella. It is also known as the common chaser, or swampwatcher. It is common through much of its range, which stretches through parts of South Asia, Southeast Asia, and Oceania, including in countries such as India, Indonesia, China, Australia, and Vietnam. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Pallid Fish Eagle,** also known as Pallas’s sea eagle or band-tailed fish eagle, is a large, brownish sea eagle. It can be seen near lakes, marshes and large rivers, from lowlands to 5,000 metres of elevation. It feeds primarily on fish, but many other prey are part of its diet. It breeds usually near water in a large nest placed in a tall tree. **It is partially migratory**, with Central Asian birds wintering among the southern Asian birds in northern India, and also further west to the Persian Gulf. **IUCN Red List: Endangered**. **So, pair III is not correctly matched.**
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:**
● A biodiversity survey has, for the first time, recorded the presence of Beddome’s cat skink (Ristella beddomii) at the Aralam and Kottiyoor Wildlife sanctuaries.
● At the Yamuna Biodiversity Park, Yellow-tailed Ashy Skimmer (Potamarcha congener) has been recorded for the first time during a recent Dragonfly survey in Delhi.
● Recently, a survey conducted jointly by the State Forest Department and the World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) has revealed rare nesting of Pallid Fish Eagle in Corbett Tiger Reserve.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Opium Poppy Cultivation in
India, consider the following statements:
I. - It can be legally cultivated only in Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh.
II. 	 The cultivators are required to tender their
entire produce to the Government.
III. 	 India is the only country authorised by the
United Nations Single Convention on Narcotic Drugs (1961) to produce opium gum.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**: Opium poppy plant is the **source of opium gum** which contains **several indispensable alkaloids** (naturally occurring organic nitrogen-containing compounds) such as morphine, codeine and thebaine. **Morphine is** **commonly used analgesic** (pain relieving medicine) while **codeine is used in manufacture of cough syrups**. The Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) **Act** **empowers the Central Government** to permit and regulate cultivation of opium poppy for medical and scientific purposes.
● **Legal cultivation is carried out in India in selected tracts** notified by the Central Government annually **in the States of Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh**. Licences are issued by the Central Bureau of Narcotics to eligible cultivators in the above three States as per the General Conditions relating to Grant of Licence framed by the Central Government. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **The cultivators are required to tender their entire opium produced to the Central Bureau of** **Narcotics (CBN)** and they are paid a price at the rates decided by the Government.  **So, statement II is** **correct.**
● **India is the only country authorized by the UN Single Convention on Narcotic Drugs, 1961,** to legally produce opium gum. 11 other countries cultivate opium poppy, but **do not extract gum**. **So, statement** **III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Government announced Annual licensing policy for cultivation of opium poppy for crop year 202526 for Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
The M23 rebel group is a militant organization
primarily active in which country?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**: **The March 23 Movement (M23) is a rebel group primarily active in the eastern Democratic Republic of** **Congo (DRC)** that is known for seizing territory, committing widespread human rights abuses, and contributing to a major humanitarian crisis. After lying dormant for years, the M23 resurged in 2021 and escalated its offensive in 2025, taking control of the strategic provincial capitals of Goma and Bukavu.
● M23 takes its name from the **March 23, 2009 peace agreement** signed between its predecessor, the National Congress for the Defense of the People (CNDP), and the DRC government. M23 was formed by ex-CNDP fighters who rebelled in 2012, claiming the government had failed to honor the agreement.
● M23 claims to defend the interests of the **ethnic Tutsi population** in the eastern DRC, which includes protecting them from Hutu militias. However, M23's agenda has expanded to include the overthrow of the Congolese government, and critics argue the group uses ethnic grievances as a pretext for economic control.
● The United Nations and other international bodies have provided extensive evidence that **neighboring** **Rwanda backs M23** with weapons, logistical support, and thousands of troops. Rwanda denies these allegations, but observers widely believe its involvement is driven by economic interests in the DRC's mineral resources. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Peace deal between DRC and Rwanda, mediated by the USA  is in trouble due to the active role of M23 Rebel group.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Wildlife Conservation (Tiger, Elephant, Lion, Rhino, Crocodile, Vulture, Tortoise, Dolphin, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which of the following protected areas in India are
home to cheetahs?

options.a:
Kuno National Park and Gandhi Sagar Wildlife
Sanctuary
options.b:
Indravati National Park and Pench National
Park
options.c:
Gandhi Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary and Pench
National Park
options.d:
Kuno National Park and Kanger Valley National
Park

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● **Kuno National Park (Madhya Pradesh)** was the primary release site for the first batches of cheetahs translocated from Namibia and South Africa, beginning in 2022.
● **Madhya Pradesh** wildlife officials have initiated plans to introduce a female cheetah to the **Gandhi** **Sagar wildlife sanctuary**. The move is part of the state’s ambitious plans to populate the site as a second home for cheetahs. In April, two cheetahs were relocated from Kuno to Gandhi Sagar, originally brought from South Africa in 2023.
o **Gandhi Sagar wildlife sanctuary** is spread across 2,500 square km, abutting Rajasthan, and is a mix of grasslands, dry deciduous forests, and riverine evergreen patches — an ideal habitat for cheetahs. It has a carrying capacity of 10 cheetahs, at present. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Project Cheetah**
● The **cheetah is listed as Vulnerable** by the World Conservation Union (IUCN) Red List of Threatened Species.
o **Two subspecies,** the Asiatic cheetah (**Acinonyx jubatus venaticus**) and the
Northwest African cheetah (**Acinonyx jubatus heck**i) are listed as **Critically** **Endangered**.
● The Asiatic cheetah is nearly extinct in its entire Asian range, except for a remnant population in Iran, about 20 individuals or less.
● The cheetah got completely wiped out in the early 1950s, mainly due to over-hunting and habitat loss.
● The primary authority that manages and oversees Project Cheetah is NTCA, under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
● Project Cheetah began with the intercontinental translocation of African cheetahs from Namibia and South Africa to the Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh.
● This translocation took place in two batches of eight and then 12 cheetahs.
● The key aims are rejuvenating the local ecosystem and enhancing biodiversity.
**Relevance**: Madhya Pradesh government is planning for a Cheetah mating programme in Gandhi Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Unified Pension Scheme
(UPS), consider the following statements:
I. - Central government employees utilizing the benefits of the National Pension System are barred from UPS.
II. 	 Registration under UPS is mandatory for all
central government employees joining from April 1, 2025.
III. 	 There is an assured payout of 50% of the
average basic pay of the last 12 months subject to completion of 25 years in service.
IV. 	 It is not applicable to already retired employees.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The Finance Ministry notified the **Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) on 24** January **2024**, as an option under the **National Pension System (NPS)** for central government employees who joined on or after January 1, 2004. UPS guarantees a fixed pension, while those employed after that were under the NPS, which is market-linked.
o The **Department of Pension and Pensioners’ Welfare** notified the Central Civil Services (Implementation of the Unified Pension Scheme under the National Pension System) Rules, 2025.
● **While the NPS was mandatory for all** **central government employees** joining from January 1, 2004, the **UPS is optional.** According to the rules notified by the department, **employees who opt for the UPS** **till September 30 2025, have a one-time option of going back to the NPS.** They can exercise this option up to one year before superannuating or three months before opting for Voluntary Retirement Scheme (VRS). Once this choice is made, the employee cannot opt for the UPS again. **So, statements I and II** **are not correct.**
● **There is an assured payout of 50% of the average basic pay of the last 12 months** subject to completion of 25 years in service under UPS. **The NPS does not have an assured amount**; it is based on the accumulated corpus. **So, statement III is correct.**
o **A minimum payout of Rs10,000 per month is guaranteed after 10 years of service under UPS.** o Contribution Structure
● Employee Contribution: 10% of Basic Pay + Dearness Allowance (DA).
● Government Contribution: Matching 10% (from payroll system).
● Additional Government Contribution: An estimated 8.5% of Basic Pay + DA to a Pool Corpus.
● UPS operates within the existing NPS architecture regulated by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) and is **applicable to both serving and retired employees under** **specific conditions**. **So, statement IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Department of Pension and Pensioners’ Welfare on September 2 notified the Central Civil Services (Implementation of the Unified Pension Scheme under the National Pension System) Rules, 2025.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the ‘Swasth Nari Sashakt Parivar’
Abhiyan, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It aims to organise more than one lakh health
camps.
II. 	 It intends to ensure comprehensive screening
for women’s health issues.
III. 	The campaign expands participation through
Nikshay Mitras.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The Swasth Nari Sashakt Parivar Abhiyaan** (**SNSPA**) is a landmark initiative by the **Ministry of** **Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) and the Ministry of Women and Child Development** **(MoWCD),** aimed at strengthening healthcare services for women and children across India, with a **focus** **on improving access, quality care, and awareness.**
o **It is described as a Jan Bhagidaari Abhiyaan**, it encourages active participation from private hospitals and healthcare professionals to foster inclusive healthcare delivery. The Abhiyan is **organising more than one lakh health camps**, making this the **largest ever health outreach for** **women** and children in the country. These camps will be organised at Ayushman Arogya Mandirs, Community Health Centres. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Swasth Nari Sashakt Parivar Abhiyaan** focuses on maternal and child care to strengthen family wellbeing, integrating with initiatives like Poshan 2.0. It prioritises women’s health by providing comprehensive health screenings and services tailored to their needs.
o Through health camps and other related initiatives, the campaign offers screenings for conditions like **dermatological concerns,** **hypertension, anaemia, reproductive health** **issues, breast and cervical cancer, diabetes,** **tuberculosis (TB), and sickle cell disease** **(SCD)**, with specialised counselling in tribal areas. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Volunteer and Nikshay Mitra Engagement:** The campaign expands participation through **Nikshay** **Mitras** and volunteers, who support tuberculosis eradication efforts and community health initiatives.
● Collaborations with voluntary organisations and the **Indian Red Cross Society**, particularly for blood donation camps, enhance community involvement. This approach, rooted in the Jan Bhagidaari Abhiyaan model, ensures sustainable impact by mobilising local support and resources. **So, statement III is correct.**
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: PM Modi Launched ‘Swasth Nari Sashakt Parivar’ Abhiyan on September 16, 2025.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Community State/Union Territory
I. - Shompen Andaman and Nicobar
II. - Moran Assam
III. - Dongria Kondh Chhattisgarh
IV. - Koraga Tamil Nadu
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The Shompen** is a **Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group** (**PVTG**), indigenous to the interior of **Great** **Nicobar Island.** **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● The **Moran** are an ethnic group primarily concentrated in the districts of Upper **Assam** and adjoining areas of **Arunachal Pradesh**. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Dongria Kondh is a PVTG** primarily found in the Niyamgiri Hills across the Rayagada and Kalahandi districts of **Odisha**.
o **The Kapdaganda Shawl**, a handwoven and embroidered textile by the Dongria Kondh tribe, received a **Geographical Indication** (GI) tag in early 2024, recognizing its unique craft, origin in Odisha's **Niyamgiri hills**, and cultural significance. **So, pair III is not correctly matched.**
● The **Koraga is a PVTG** found mainly in the coastal districts of **Karnataka** (Dakshina Kannada and Udupi) and **Kerala** (Kasaragod). They speak the Tulu language and have matrilineal inheritance. **So, pair** **IV is not correctly matched.** **So, only two of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The Moran community recently launched an 'economic blockade' in Tinsukia, Assam, intensifying their demand for Scheduled Tribe status.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water in the Atmosphere (Clouds, Condensation, Precipitation, Rainfall, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following methods:
I. 	 High-altitude wetlands
II. 	 Monolithic concrete embankments and levees
III. 	Reforestation with broad-leaved trees
IV. 	 Contour trenching
Which of the above can minimize the impact of
flooding in the Himalayas?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Green methods, also known as **Nature-Based Solutions (NBS)** or green infrastructure, focus on using natural processes to manage flood risk in the Himalayas. These strategies are a sustainable and cost-effective alternative to traditional engineering, helping to absorb excess water, stabilize fragile slopes, and build climate resilience in vulnerable communities.
● **Wetlands** act as natural sponges, absorbing large volumes of meltwater and rainwater and releasing it slowly. This reduces the peak flow velocity and volume, effectively minimizing flash floods. **So, point I** **is correct.**
● The **use of monolithic concrete embankments and levees** is a structural flood management method that creates rigid, vertical or near-vertical barriers to contain a river or prevent water from inundating an adjacent area. **Monolithic concrete** embankments and **levees are man-made infrastructure** solutions. **So, point II is not correct.**
● **Tree canopies** and root systems intercept and absorb rainwater, slow surface runoff, and stabilize slopes. **Broad-leaved species are effective in mountain ecology for retaining soil moisture and preventing** **erosion/landslides**, which often trigger floods. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Contour trenching** is a highly effective, low-cost green method of soil and water conservation used primarily on hill slopes and degraded lands.
o It involves digging trenches along the contour lines of a hill slope. It is a soil and water conservation technique that captures runoff water, prevents soil erosion, and allows for infiltration, thereby reducing the volume of water moving downstream rapidly. **So, point IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Dehradun and several other districts in Uttarakhand have experienced very heavy rainfall over the past few days, triggering landslides in multiple areas and causing rivers to swell to dangerous levels.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Bima Sugam:
I. 	 It is a unified digital marketplace for insurance
products and services.
II. 	 It is managed by the Insurance Regulatory
Development Authority of India.
III. 	It will cover life insurance but not health
insurance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Bima Sugam**, envisioned as the **world’s largest online marketplace for insurance products and** **services. It** aspires to be part of India’s **Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI);** envisioned to enable seamless, paperless, and trusted access to insurance products and services for every Indian citizen. **So,** **statement I is correct**
o The initiative to the larger national vision of **‘Insurance for All by 2047’**, part of the Prime Minister’s mission of Viksit Bharat 2047. o The Bima Sugam platform will empower customers, intermediaries and agents to seamlessly find the right coverage, buy, sell and manage multiple policies, offerings, insurance awareness, education and a frictionless experience.
● **Bima Sugam** is managed by **Bima Sugam India Federation (BSIF)** under the aegis of IRDAI and has been established as a “not-for-profit company” **under section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013**. **So,** **statement II is not correct**.
o IRDAI with the objectives to empower and protect the interest of policyholders, to increase penetration of insurance in India and to enhance accessibility, issued the IRDAI (Bima Sugam Insurance Electronic Marketplace) Regulations 2024.
● **The spectrum of offerings on the platform will be wide and diverse**. It will cover life insurance in all its forms, including term plans, savings products, annuity and pension plans, as well as U**LIP (unit-linked** **insurance plan)** products. **It will also provide health insurance options,** addressing the full range of medical and wellness needs. **So, statement III is not correct**.
o **For vehicles**, the marketplace will **offer motor insurance**, covering both third-party liability and own-damage policies. Individuals and families will have access to **travel insurance and personal** **accident insurance**, while businesses will benefit from **a variety of property and commercial** **insurance products**, including marine, agricultural, and industry-specific covers. In addition, the platform will remain flexible to host any new insurance products. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The Bima Sugam portal was launched at the headquarters of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) in Hyderabad, to realise the goal of Insurance for every Indian.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
The term ‘Snapback Mechanism’, sometimes
seen in the news, is associated with:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The United Nations has reimposed wide-ranging sanctions on Iran over its nuclear programme. **The sanctions—** **revived through a “snapback” mechanism**.
● **Snapback Mechanism** was included in the **Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA)**, which was signed in July 2015 between Iran and the P5+1 group (China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, the United States, plus Germany) and was subsequently endorsed by the UN Security Council Resolution 2231 (2015).
● In September 2025, France, Germany, and the United Kingdom triggered the **snapback** after accusing Iran of restricting monitoring by the International Atomic Energy Agency and failing to account for its growing stockpile of highly enriched uranium.
● **The Snapback Mechanism** serves as an emergency enforcement tool. It allows any participant state to unilaterally initiate the swift re-imposition (or 'snap back') of all prior United Nations sanctions if they believe **Iran** has committed a "significant non-performance" of its nuclear commitments. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: European countries have reintroduced sanctions against Iran following UN snapback.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following applications:
I. 	 Protein purification
II. 	 Separation of active pharmaceutical ingredients
III. 	Assessing pollution in water
IV. 	 Separating DNA and RNA In how many of the above Ion Exchange Chromatography is used?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Chromatography** is a laboratory technique used for separating mixtures into their individual components. **Ion Exchange Chromatography** separates ions and polar molecules based on their affinity to ion exchangers. It is used to detect pollutants, pesticides, and other contaminants in water and soil samples.
● **Applications of Ion exchange chromatography:** o **Protein purification:** Ion exchange chromatography is commonly used to purify proteins from complex mixtures. By selecting the appropriate stationary phase and elution conditions, specific proteins can be separated and purified with high purity and yield. **So, point I is correct.** o **Pharmaceutical industry:** In drug development, ion exchange chromatography is employed to **separate and purify active pharmaceutical ingredients**, ensuring high quality and safety of medications. **So, point II is correct.** o **Environmental analysis:** This technique plays a crucial role in **water and soil analysis** by separating and quantifying ions, heavy metals, and other pollutants, aiding in environmental monitoring and regulatory compliance. **So, point III is correct.** o **Nucleic acid separation:** Ion exchange chromatography is utilized for **separating DNA and RNA** fragments based on their charge characteristics. This is essential in molecular biology research and genetic analysis. **So, point IV is correct.** o **Biotechnology:** Ion exchange chromatography is often a part of downstream processing in biotechnology, helping to isolate and purify biomolecules like enzymes, antibodies, and hormones. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Recently, University of Tasmania scientists developed a portable ion chromatograph for field use, enhancing student learning and real-world applications.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Universal Immunisation Programme aims
to provide vaccination to prevent eleven vaccine-preventable diseases nationwide.
II.	 Mission Indradhanush was launched as a
special drive to vaccinate all unvaccinated and partially vaccinated children.
III. 	Intensified Mission Indradhanush ensures
that routine immunization services reach the dropped-out children and pregnant women.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **India’s Universal Immunisation Programme (UIP)** is the world’s largest, and annually vaccinates 2.6 crore infants and 2.9 crore pregnant women.
o Under the Universal Immunization Programme, the Government of India is providing **vaccination** **to prevent eleven vaccine-preventable diseases nationwide** i.e. Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus, Polio, Measles, Rubella, severe form of Childhood Tuberculosis, Hepatitis B, Haemophilus Influenza type B (Hib), Pneumococcal and Diarrhoeas due to Rota virus **and one vaccine in** **endemic areas**. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Mission Indradhanush (MI)**, **launched in December 2014,** as a special drive to vaccinate all unvaccinated and partially vaccinated children under UIP, with a goal to reach 90% coverage. Mission Indradhanush specifically focuses on areas with low immunization rates, including hard-to-reach regions and communities where children are either unvaccinated or partially vaccinated. **So, statement II is** **correct.**
● In 2017, the **Intensified Mission Indradhanush (IMI)** programme was launched to strategically **target** **low-coverage areas and missed populations.** By 2023, 12 phases of the MI and IMI had been completed, vaccinating 5.46 crore children and 1.32 crore pregnant women. The MI and IMI phases were integrated into campaigns such as the Gram Swaraj Abhiyan and the Extended Gram Swaraj Abhiyan to further the outreach. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Routine immunization sessions are organized on a weekly basis in rural and urban areas of the district to vaccinate children and pregnant women.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Air, Water, Noise Pollution, SLCPs, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Happy Seeder
II. 	 Ozone fumigation
III. 	Biochar production
IV. 	 Pusa decomposer
V. 	 Pelletization
How many of the above technological interventions can control stubble burning?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The Happy Seeder** is a tractor-mounted machine that cuts and lifts rice straw, sows’ wheat into the soil, then mulches the sown area with the straw. **It was developed by the Punjab Agriculture University** **(PAU)** in collaboration with the **Australian Centre for International Agri Research (ACIAR)**. It is also known as the Turbo Happy Seeder. **So, point I is correct.**
o  The happy seeder technology is eco-friendly with an environment for the health of the soil as well as it saves water. It also removes the need to burn rice stubble before planting wheat, therefore reducing air pollution.
● **Ozone fumigation or Ozone treatment** uses ozone (O₃), a powerful oxidant, to disinfect, sterilize, and remove contaminants from spaces and materials like clean rooms, grains, and other agricultural products.
o **It's a green technology effective against bacteria, viruses, molds, and pests,** which reverts to harmless oxygen after treatment, leaving no harmful residues, and thus improving food safety, product quality, and extending shelf life. **So, point II is not correct.**
● **Producing biochar from stubble** is a sustainable alternative to burning it, which releases harmful carbon into the atmosphere. **This method locks carbon into the soi**l, offering a long-term solution to both waste management and carbon sequestration. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Pusa Decomposer,** a microbial spray, was developed by the Indian Agriculture Research Institute (IARI), Delhi. Pusa bio-decomposer is a microbial spray (consortium of 7 different micro-organisms) which helps to decompose the crop residues in a short span of time and thereby, clear the land for the next crop. **So,** **point IV is correct.**
● **Crop residue** **can be used as fuel to generate electricity** through biomass-based power plants. **Biomass** **pellets** reduce stubble burning by converting agricultural waste into a valuable, clean-burning fuel that can be used in power plants, industrial boilers, and even for heating and cooking. **So, point V is correct.**
**So, only four of the five technologies can control stubble burning.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The Bench of Chief Justice B.R. Gavai and Justice K.V. Chandran suggested that the Union consider penal provisions to tackle stubble burning.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs with regard to India’s
foundational AI projects:
Initiative Related
I. - Zenteiq Reasoning model for STEM disciplines
II. - Intellihealth Screening of neurological disorders
III. - GenLoop Small language models
IV. - Shodh AI Applications in agriculture and finance
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Under the IndiaAI Foundation Models pillar, eight pioneering projects have been launched to build indigenous AI models trained on India-specific data. Selected from about 506 proposals, these initiatives span multilingual and domain-specific models, scientific discovery, healthcare, and industrial innovation laying the groundwork for India’s AI leadership. **These 8 pillars are**:
● **Avatar A**I – Creating specialized “AI Avatars” optimized for Indian languages and domains such as agriculture, healthcare, and governance.
● **Zenteiq** is developing BrahmAI, a science-driven multimodal foundation model to advance engineering intelligence, scientific computing, and industrial innovation. **So, pair I is not correctly matched.**
● **IIT Bombay Consortium – Bharat Gen** is developing multilingual and multimodal models with an opensource approach to support applications in agriculture, finance, legal, health, and education.
● **Intellihealth** is proposing a model for EEG signal analysis to enable early screening of neurological disorders and advance brain–computer interface research. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Fractal Analytics Ltd.** is Building India’s first large reasoning model designed for structured reasoning, STEM disciplines, and medical problem-solving.
● **Tech Mahindra Maker’s Lab** is designing an efficient model for Indic languages (with a focus on Hindi dialects), alongside an agentic AI platform, Orion, for government applications.
● **GenLoop** is creating small language models– Yukti (Base), Varta (Instruction), and Kavach (Guard) – to support all 22 scheduled Indian languages with native reasoning and content moderation. **So, pair III is** **correctly matched.**
● **Shodh AI** is developing a model for material discovery, integrating AI into experimental workflows to accelerate innovation in material sciences. **So, pair IV is not correctly matched.** **So, only two of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology, Government of India, unveiled the logo and key flagship initiatives for the India-AI Impact Summit 2026.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: India and Neighbourhood (Pakistan, China, Nepal, Sri Lanka, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Chabahar Port, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It is governed by a trilateral agreement
between India, Iran, and Afghanistan.
II. 	 India’s development of Chabahar port is
exempted from the CAATSA Act sanctions by the USA.
III. 	It is not part of the proposed International
North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Chabahar is a deep-water port** located in the **Sistan-Baluchistan** province of **Iran**.  It is the closest Iranian port to India and is located in the open sea, providing easy and secure access for large cargo ships. In May 2016, **a Trilateral Agreement to** establish the International Transport and Transit Corridor (Chabahar Agreement) **was signed** by India, Iran, and Afghanistan. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The United States has said it is withdrawing the sanctions waiver granted to **Iran’s Chabahar port**, which is being developed by India, thereby ending the 2018 waiver provided during the first term (2017-2021) of President Donald Trump. The **Countering America’s Adversaries Through Sanctions Act** **(CAATSA)**  imposes new sanctions on Iran, Russia, and North Korea. Earlier, the USA granted a
sanctions waiver for India s development of Chabahar Port and the S-400 defence deal with Russia. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The port is also part of the proposed International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC), a multi-modal transportation project linking the Indian Ocean and Persian Gulf to the Caspian Sea via Iran, and onward to northern Europe via St Petersburg in Russia. **So, statement III is not** **correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: The US administration led by President Donald Trump had withdrawn the sanctions waiver granted to the Chabahar port in Iran.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
The Venice Charter, adopted in 1964, established
an international framework for:

options.a:
The conservation and restoration of cultural
heritage
options.b:
The conservation and protection of migratory
birds
options.c:
The conservation and protection of threatened
animals
options.d:
The conservation and restoration of freshwater
bodies

questions.explanationMarkdown:
The UNESCO World Heritage Convention recognises sites of outstanding cultural and natural value across the globe.
● Seven natural heritage sites across India, including the Deccan Traps in Maharashtra, **the geological** **heritage of St Mary’s Island in Karnataka,** Meghalaya’s Age Caves, **Naga Hill Ophiolite in Nagaland,** **Erra Matti Dibbalu in Andhra Pradesh, the natural heritage of Tirumala Hills in Andhra Pradesh** **and the Varkala Cliffs in Kerala**, have been added to UNESCO’s Tentative List of World Heritage Sites, taking the country’s tally on the list to 69.
● The **Venice Charter established an international framework for the conservation and restoration of** **cultural heritage**. At the 2nd International Congress of Architects and Technicians of Historic Monuments in Venice,  1964, a resolution put forward by UNESCO, called for the creation of an International Council on Monuments and Sites, paving the way for the founding of ICOMOS.
○ ICOMOS, the International Council on Monuments and Sites, is a non-governmental organisation working to conserve and protect cultural heritage sites around the world. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Seven natural heritage sites across India have been added to UNESCO’s Tentative List of World Heritage Sites, taking the country’s tally on the list to 69.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
The government of India has launched the NE-
SPARKS initiative to:

options.a: Assist start-ups in the defence sector
options.c: Expose science students to ISRO facilities

questions.explanationMarkdown:
The Government of India has been focusing on promoting scientific temper and space awareness among students, especially from underrepresented regions such as the North East.
● In this context, the **Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (MDoNER), in collaboration** **with the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO),** launched the North East Students’ Programme for Awareness, Reach, and Knowledge on Space (NE-SPARKS) in 2024.
● Under this program, **the Ministry of DoNER will facilitate the visit of 800 young and talented science** **stream students (100 from each NE State) to ISRO** with the support of respective state governments. It is aimed at igniting curiosity and fostering awareness about space science and technology among students from the North Eastern Region (NER) of India. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The launch of NE-SPARKS was announced in September 2024, to bridge geographical and informational gaps by providing students with an immersive experience of India's advancements in space research and exploration through visits to ISRO Centre in Bengaluru.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
National Initiative on Water Security:
I. 	 A fixed portion of funds will now be
mandatorily spent on water conservation works under MGNREGA.
II. 	 The Central Ground Water Board is authorised
to supervise the water conservation works.
III. 	It aims to provide potable water to every rural
household through tap water.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The **Ministry of Rural Development** and the **Jal Shakti Ministry** have jointly launched the ‘National Initiative on Water Security’.
● Under the **National Initiative on Water Security** **a fixed portion of MGNREGA funds must be** **earmarked for water conservation**. Following this directive, it has now been mandated that:
o In ‘over-exploited’ blocks, 65% of MGNREGA funds will be used for water-related works. o In ‘semi-critical’ blocks, 40% of MGNREGA funds will be spent on water conservation. o Even in blocks without water scarcity, at least 30% of funds will go towards water-related works. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The funds for the initiative come from the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), which is under the Ministry of Rural Development. **While the Central Ground Water** **Board, jointly with the State Ground Water Departments, carries out periodic assessment of ground** **water resources of the entire country.**
o The National Initiative on Water Security is supervised by the Union Ministry of Jal Shakti and the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● **Jal Jeevan Mission-Har Ghar Jal** is being implemented by the Government of India, in partnership with States since August 2019, which aims at providing potable water in adequate quantity (55 litres per capita per day) of prescribed quality on a regular and long-term basis to every rural household through tap water connection. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Minister for Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Jal Shakti Minister C R Patil jointly launched the National Initiative on Water Security in New Delhi.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Thali Index is a metric to measure food
affordability in India.
II. 	 Roti Rice Rate, by CRISIL, a monthly
indicator of food plate cost.
III. 	Under the Public Distribution System,
only rice, wheat and sugar are provided to beneficiaries.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Food security in India is no longer just about filling stomachs with calories, but about ensuring nutrition for all sections of society.
● The Thali Index has been used as a tool to measure whether families can afford a simple balanced meal. The "thali index" is a proposed metric to measure poverty and food affordability in India by tracking the cost and availability of a basic, culturally relevant vegetarian "thali" (a meal platter). **The Thali Index** **estimates the cost of preparing a simple, balanced “thali” with cereals, pulses, vegetables, oil, and** **other basics**. **So, statement I is correct.**
○ In 2023-24, up to 40 per cent of the rural population and 10 per cent of urban population could not afford two thalis a day, indicating that levels of food deprivation are much higher than implied by poverty estimates.
● **Roti Rice Rate:** It is **CRISIL’s monthly indicator of food plate cost.** The average cost of preparing a thali at home is calculated based on input prices prevailing in north, south, east and west India. The monthly change reflects the impact on the common man’s expenditure. The data also reveals the ingredients (cereals, pulses, broilers, vegetables, spices, edible oil and cooking gas) driving the change in the cost of the thali. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Current PDS benefits are heavily tilted towards rice and wheat**, which sometimes reach even relatively better-off families. Policy experts suggest reducing excessive cereal subsidies for such groups and reallocating that money to pulses and other nutritious foods. This would make the PDS more nutritionsensitive and better aligned with the Thali Index findings.
○ Under the PDS, presently the commodities namely wheat, rice, sugar and kerosene are being allocated to the States/UTs for distribution. **Some States/UTs also distribute additional items of** **mass consumption through the PDS outlets such as pulses, edible oils, iodized salt, spices, etc**. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recent studies using the Thali Index show that nearly half of rural households and a significant share of urban households still cannot afford two balanced meals daily.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Manual scavenging is a violation of human
dignity under Article 14.
II. 	 The NAMASTE scheme aims to achieve zero
fatalities in sanitation work in India.
III. 	Swachhata Abhiyaan mobile application
captures the data on manual scavengers.
IV. 	 The State Governments are responsible for
the rehabilitation of manual scavengers.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The practice of manual scavenging and hazardous sewer cleaning has been a longstanding social evil in India, deeply rooted in caste-based discrimination. “Prohibition of Employment as Manual Scavengers and their Rehabilitation Act, 2013 (**MS Act, 2013**)” manual scavenging is a prohibited activity in the country with effect from 2013.
● In the Dr. Balram Singh v/s Union of India case, the Supreme Court emphasised that **manual scavenging** **is a violation of human dignity under Article 21** and instructed both the Union and State Governments to take concrete measures to eliminate the practice. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● To tackle hazardous cleaning specially, the Government has formulated a new scheme called **‘National** **Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem’ (NAMASTE).** NAMASTE aims to achieve the following outcomes:
○ **Zero fatalities in sanitation work in India. So, statement II is correct.** ○ All sanitation work to be performed by formalized skilled workers ○ No sanitation workers to come in direct contact with human faecal matter ○ Sanitation workers are collectivized into SHGs and are empowered to run sanitation enterprises.
● Under the Swachh Bharat Mission, the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has launched a mobile app “Swachhata Abhiyaan” in 2020 **to capture the data of insanitary latrines still existing and** **manual scavengers associated with their cleaning**. Any person can upload the data of insanitary latrines and manual scavengers on the mobile app. Thereafter, the data is verified by the concerned district administration. **So, statement III is correct.**
● Section 13 (2) of the MS Act 2023 says that, **the District Magistrate of the district concerned shall be** **responsible for rehabilitation of each manual scavenger** in accordance with the provisions of subsection (1) and the State Government or the District Magistrate concerned may, in addition, assign responsibilities in his behalf to officers subordinate to the District Magistrate and to officers of the concerned Municipality. **So, statement IV is correct.** **So, only three of the four statements are correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Delhi Public Works Department has been fined Rs 5 lakh by the Supreme Court after its labourers were found cleaning sewers outside the court building manually -- a process that is banned in view of the risks it poses to workers.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Bagram Air Base, recently seen in the news, is
located in:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Bagram Air Base, also called Bagram Airfield, is located in Parwan Province of Afghanistan**, around 50 km north of the capital city Kabul.
● It is Afghanistan’s largest military base and airfield, and historically one of the most strategically important installations in Central Asia. The base has two runways, multiple hangars, and extensive facilities, making it capable of handling large-scale military operations.
● Bagram Air Base was first built during the Cold War and played a crucial role in the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan (1979–1989), serving as a major operational hub. Following the U.S.-led invasion in 2001, it became the central base for American forces, hosting troops, aircraft, drones, and detention facilities, and later functioned as the command centre for the NATO-led ISAF mission.
● In July 2021, the U.S. handed it over to Afghan forces, but within weeks the Taliban seized control, marking the end of U.S. dominance in Afghanistan and symbolising a major geopolitical shift in the region. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Four years after the last helicopter lifted off in 2021, President Trump is demanding the base back from the Taliban government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.microTopic: UT, Scheduled Areas, Other Scheduled Areas
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Sixth Schedule areas in India:
I. 	 Autonomous District Councils formed under
the Sixth Schedule can have up to 30 members with a fixed term of five years.
II. 	 In these areas, people from other parts of the
country had been restricted from acquiring land.
III. 	A state having more than 90% of tribal
population is considered eligible for inclusion in the Sixth Schedule.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **VI Schedule of the Indian Constitution, established under Articles 244(2) and 275(1)**, provides for the administration and self-governance of tribal areas in the Northeastern states of **Assam**, **Meghalaya**, **Mizoram** (three Councils each), and **Tripura** (one Council) through Autonomous District Councils (ADCs) and Regional Councils.
● **The Sixth Schedule under Article 244** provides for the formation of autonomous administrative divisions — **Autonomous District Councils (ADCs)** — that have some legislative, judicial, and administrative autonomy within a state.
o **ADCs have up to 30 members** with a term of five years, and can make laws, rules and regulations with regard to land, forest, water, agriculture, village councils, health, sanitation, village- and town-level policing, inheritance, marriage and divorce, social customs and mining, etc. **So,** **statement I is correct.** o **The Bodoland Territorial Council in Assam** is an exception with more than 40 members and the right to make laws on 39 issues.
● **People from other parts of the country had been restricted from purchasing or acquiring land there,** and its distinct cultural heritage needed preservation. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The objectives of the Sixth Schedule of the Indian constitution are to protect tribal land and resources and prohibit the transfer of such resources to non-tribal individuals or communities and to ensure the tribal communities are not exploited or marginalized by non-tribal populations and that their cultural and social identities are preserved and promoted.
o No region outside the Northeast has been included in the Sixth Schedule. In fact, even in Manipur,
which has predominantly tribal populations in some places, the autonomous councils are not included in the Sixth Schedule. **Nagaland and Arunachal Pradesh, which are totally tribal, are** **also not in the Sixth Schedule**. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: People of Ladakh are protesting to include Ladakh into the Sixth Schedule.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Wildlife Conservation (Tiger, Elephant, Lion, Rhino, Crocodile, Vulture, Tortoise, Dolphin, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Red Panda in India, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 They are found in West Bengal and
Meghalaya.
II. 	 They are listed under Schedule III of the
Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972.
III. 	Red Panda Conservation Breeding Project
is being carried out at Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park, Darjeeling.
How many of the above statements are **not** correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The Red Panda is a small arboreal mammal native to the eastern Himalayas and adjacent mountain ranges. Though often overshadowed by the giant panda, it is an ecologically important species, feeding mainly on bamboo and playing a role in seed dispersal and forest ecosystem health.
● In India, Red Pandas are distributed across the eastern Himalayan states, including **Sikkim, West Bengal** **(especially the Singalila National Park), Arunachal Pradesh, and parts of Meghalaya**. These areas provide dense temperate forests with abundant bamboo, which forms the staple diet of the species. Their distribution is highly fragmented due to terrain and human encroachment. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **The Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, places the Red Panda in Schedule I,** which ensures maximum protection under Indian law. Hunting, trading, or disturbing Red Pandas or their habitats attracts the strictest penalties under the Act. **So, statement II is not correct.**
○ **Schedule III** of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972 lists animals for which hunting is prohibited, but they receive less protection than species in Schedule I and II, with lower penalties for violations.
● In India, **Red Panda Conservation Breeding Project at Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park,** **Darjeeling** is the only breeding programme for the Red Panda in its natural distribution zone. Its mission is:
○ To breed genetically and demographically viable red pandas in captivity with the aim to augment the ex-situ population when the need arises. ○ To create awareness of the importance of conservation of the species. ○ To facilitate research on the biology, management, and in-situ and ex-situ conservation of the species. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, under a conservation programme at the Himalayan Zoological Park near Sikkim’s capital, Gangtok red panda cubs were born after a 7 years gap.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: National Park, Wildlife Sanctuary, Biosphere Reserve
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements is **not** correct
about the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve?

options.a: It is located in Himachal Pradesh.
options.b:
It is not a part of the world network of
biosphere reserves by UNESCO.
options.c:
It is characterized by barren lands, high-
altitude deserts, and alpine meadows.
options.d:
It is home to snow leopard, Tibetan antelope,
and Himalayan wolf.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve** is a biosphere reserve in the Western Himalayas, Himachal Pradesh, India. It is **stretched along the Western Himalayas, from Ladakh to Kinnaur, in Himachal Pradesh**. It spans approximately 7,770 sq. km. across the landscapes of Himachal Pradesh’s Lahaul-Spiti district.
● **Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve** is India’s **first high altitude Cold desert biosphere reserve** that has been **included in the World network of biosphere reserves by UNESCO on 27 Sep, 2025.**
● Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve is distinguished by its starkly beautiful landscape, **characterized by** **barren lands, high-altitude deserts, and alpine meadows (not by Taiga Biome)**.
● It is home to many rare and endangered species, including the **snow leopard, Tibetan antelope, and** **Himalayan wolf.** It also has a variety of flora that has been used for medicinal purposes and is considered to be of Outstanding Universal Value for conservation. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
